270-18  December 11, 201 8  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   on   March   7,   2017,   the   West   Orange   Township   Council   (the  “ Council ” )   adopted   Resolution   74-17   which   authorized   the   Mayor   to   execute   an   Interim  Redevelopment   Agreement   between   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   and  the Alpert Group, LLC ( “ Alpert ” );   and  WHEREAS,   on   March   7,   2017,   the   Township   and   Alpert   entered   into   the   Interim  Redevelopment   Agreement   (the   “ Agreement ” )   wherein   Alpert   was   designated   the   Interim  Redeveloper   and   also   agreed   to   perform   demolition   services   as   part   of   a   redevelopment ;  and  WHEREAS,   on   September   4,   2018,   the   Council   adopted   Resolution   192-18   which  authorized   the   Mayor   to   execute   a   First   Amendment   to   Interim   Redevelopment   Agreement  between   Township   of   West   Orange   and   the   Alpert   Group,   LLC   (the   “ First   Amendment ” )  related to an   assignment as to the sale of another property as part of a redevelopment ; and  WHEREAS,   on   September   4,   2018,   the   Township   and   Alpert   executed   the  Agreement to incorporate the First Amendment; and  WHEREAS,   the   Agreement   and   First   Amendment   involve   certa in   Parcels   as  defined   in   the   Interim   Redevelopment   Agreement,   including   the   v acant   lot   known   as   Block  7,   Lot   22   (the   “ Selecto-Flash   Vacant   Lot ” ),   and   Block   9,   Lot   36   (the   “ Selecto-Flash  Building Property ” )   (collectively the   “ Selecto-Flash Property ” ) ;   and  WHEREAS ,   Section   2.05   of   the   Agreement,   captioned   “ Reimbursement   of  Demolition   Costs ”   provides   that   the   Alpert   shall   be   entitled   to   reimbursement   of   all  Demolition   Costs   from   any   subsequent   designated   redeveloper   or   any   subsequent   owner   of  the   Selecto- Flash   Building   Property   in   the   event   that   the   Agreement   is   terminated   by   either  Party; and  WHEREAS ,   Section   2.07   of   the   Agreement,   captioned   “ Environmental

Remediation ”   references the Township ’ s ongoing efforts to remediate the Selecto-Flash  Building   Prop erty,   including   the   Township ’ s   pursuit   of   funding   from   the   New   Jersey  Department   of   Environment   Protection   (the   “ NJDEP ” )   under   the   Hazardous   Discharge   Site  Remediation Program (the   “ HDSRP ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   Section   2.09   of   the   Agreement,   captioned   “ Redevelopm ent   Proposal ”  includes   a   provision   that   provides   Alpert   a   right   to   purchase   the   Select-Flash   Building  Property; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   has   retained   and   designated   Matrix   New   World  ( “ Matrix ” ) as its Licensed Site Remediation Professional ( “ LSRP ” ) ; and  WHEREAS ,   Matrix,   as   LSRP,   is   preparing   a   supplemental   grant   application   with  the   NJDEP   through   the   HDSRP   to   partially   fund   its   proposed   Remedial   Action   Workplan  ( “ RAWP ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   the   HDSRP   provides   75%   funding   of   costs   for   a   municipality ’ s  RAWP   and   re quires   that   the   municipality   fund   the   remaining   25%   of   costs   for   the   RAWP;  and  WHEREAS ,   the   HDSRP   requires   affirmative   confirmation   that   the   Township   will  fund the remaining 25% of the costs for the RAWP; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   and   Alpert   have   negotiated   a   Second   Amendment   to  the   Agreement   (the   “ Second Amendment ” )   annexed hereto as Exh ibit   “ A ” ;   and  WHEREAS,   the   Second   Amendment   provides   that   the   Alpert   will   fund   the  remaining   25%   of   the   costs   for   the   RAWP   with   the   ability   for   reimbursement   or   credit  towards the purchase of the Selecto Flash Building Property ;   and  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the  Township   of   West   Orange   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the  annexed   Second   Amendment   to   Interim   Redevelopment   Agreement   between   t he   Township  of West Orange and T he Alpert Group, LLC;

IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   notice   of   the   approval   and   execution   of   this  A greement   shall   be   made   in   accordance   with   l aw   and   a   copy   of   the   Second   Amendment  shall be maintained by the Municipal Clerk for the Township of West Orange.  Approval of this Resolution is acceptable to the Law  Department based upon the facts and circumstances  provided.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  Mun i cipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   December 11, 2018  Approval of this Resolution is acceptable to the Law  Department based upon the facts and circumstances  provided.  Richard D. Trenk  Township   Attorney  4811-3786-6096, v. 1

RESOLUTION 2019 SENIOR CITIZEN PUBLIC HEALTH NURSE

pages.text: 271-18  December 11, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Essex   County   Division   of   Senior   Services   has   agreed   to   provide  partial   funding   for   a   Senior   Citizen   Public   Health   Nurse   for   the   contract   period   of:   (i)  January 1,   2019   through December 31,   2019 , in the amount of $16,127;   and  WHEREAS,   the   Health   Officer   has   reviewed   the   annexed   agreement   and  recommends execution and approval   of the contract ; and  WHEREAS,   the Township has participated in this program in previous years; and  WHEREAS,   annexed here to is a copy of the   proposed   agreement;  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the   annexed  agreement   with   the   Essex   County   Division   of   Senior   Services   for   th e   time   periods  specified   herein,   in   connection   with   the   partial   funding   of   a   Senior   Citizen   Public   Health  Nurse ,   and   the   Municipal   Clerk   shall   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   attest   to   the   Mayor ’ s  signature; and it is further  RESOLVED,   that   a   permanent   cop y   of   this   agreement   shall   be   maintained   by   the  Municipal Clerk and the Department of Health.  Karen   J.   Carnevale , R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  Mun icipal   Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   December 11, 2018

272-18  Decem ber 11, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   on   April 5, 2002 ,   a mortgage was filed by the Township under the  West Orange Housing Rehabilitation Program, against   Mr.   Camillo DeMartinis   and Mrs.  Maria DeMartinis   (the Owner s )   who own   37 Thorn Terrace   in the Township of West Ora nge  known as Block   153.11 , Lot   49   (the Property); and  WHEREAS , the mortgage was in the amount of $ 11,185 .00   payable whenever this  p roperty was sold or the title transferred; and  WHEREAS , the Owner ha s   indicated that they have repaid the entire amoun t of the  m ortgage on or about   December 7 , 2018 ;   and  WHEREAS,   the Department of   Planning and Development has reviewed   their   files and  found that the mortgage filed against the Property in the amount of $ 11,185 .0 0   has been paid in  full;  NOW, BE IT RE SOLVED THAT   t he Mayor be and is hereby authorized to execute a  Discharge of Mortgage , attached hereto,   with regard to the Property   and Owners ; and   be it  further  RESOLVED   THAT   the Municipal Clerk be and is hereby authorized to attest  to the Mayor ’ s s ignature on the Discharge of M ortgage concerning the Property; and be it  further  RESOLVED   THAT   a copy of the Resolution shall be made available in the Clerk ’ s  office i n accordance with applicable law.  Karen   Carnevale,   R.M.C.   Susan McCartney , Council  President  Municipal Clerk  Adopted: December 11, 2018

pages.text: 274-18  December 11, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   an   entity   known   as   2017   Eagle   Rock   West   Orange ,   LLC   (the   “ Eagle   Rock ” )  is   the   contract   purchaser   of   the   property   located   at   662   Eagle   Rock   Avenue   and   also   known   as  a   portion   of   existing   Lots   12   and   14   in   Block   176.05   on   the   Tax   Map   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange   (the   “ Property ” ) ; and  WHEREAS,   as   a   condition   to   the   variance   and   site   plan   approvals   for   the   development  of   the   Property,   the   West   Orange   Zoning   Board   of   Adjustment   is   requiring   Eagle   Rock   to   permit  the   Township   to   use   the   P ar king   Area   during   certain   non-banking   hours   for   the   purpose   of  public parking of standard passenger vehicles ; and  WHEREAS,   Eagle   Rock   has   agreed   to   a   license   agreement,   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit  “ A ”   (the   “ License   Agreement ” )   allowing   the   Township   to   utilize   the   Parking   Area   during   certain  non-banking hours for the   purpose of public   parking ; and  WHEREAS,   Eagle Rock as agreed to grant this lice nse   at no cost to the Township; and  WHEREAS ,   Eagle   Rock   also   seeks   to   grant   an   access   agreement ,   annexed   hereto   as  Ex hibit   “ B ”   (the   “ Access   Easement ” )   to   the   Township   for   purposes   of   allowing   access   from  Eagle Rock Avenue and Pleasant Valley Way   to the Township ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   has   obtained   a   Certificate   of   Insurance   confirming   coverage  of the Property annexed   hereto as Exhibit   “ C ” ;  NOW   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   execute   the  License Agreement annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A ” ;   and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Township   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   execute   the   Access  Easement annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ B ” ; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Mayor   is   authorized   to   execute   the   License   Agreement   annexed  hereto as Exhibit   “ A ” ;   and be it further

2  RESOLVED,   that   the   Mayor   is   authorized   to   execute   the   Access   Easement   annexed  hereto as Exhibit   “ B ” ; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Township   Clerk   is   authorized   to   witness   the   Mayor ’ s   signature   on  the License Agreement; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office  in accordance with applicable law.  Karen   J.   Carnevale , R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  Municipal   Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   Dece mber 11, 2018  4823-6924-6586, v. 1

275-18  December 11, 2018  R E S O L U T I O N  ESSEX COUNTY   COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT   BLOCK GRANT   PROGRAM  YEAR   201 9  WHEREAS, certain Federal funding will potentially be available for Essex  County under Title I of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1974 as  amended and suppl emented, which Act is commonly known and referred to as the  Community Development Block Grant; and  WHEREAS, the Township of West Orange has a need and desire for assistance  under the Act and Program as aforesaid in order to improve essential services to th e  residents of areas which qualify for assistance under the Act and Program as  aforementioned.  NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED the Township Council of the  Township of West Orange, authorizes, on behalf of the Township of West Orange, the  submission of appli cations for funding of the attached list, known as the   20 1 9  Community Development Block Grant Program list, in the priority order as shown.  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED, that the Mayor of the Township of West Orange  is authorized to sign all applications and exe cute any agreements with the County of  Essex and the Municipal Clerk to attest his signature for projects to be funded under this  program.  ______________________________   _____________________________  Karen J. Carnevale,   R.M.C.   Susan McCartney, Council  P resident  Municipal Clerk  Adopted:   December 11, 2018

pages.text: 277 -18  December 11 , 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   on   or   about   September   17,   2013,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Council  authorized   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   to   enter   into   a   Contract   for   Employee  Prescription Drug Insurance (the   “ Agreement ” ) wi th Benecard Services, Inc. ( “ Benecard ” ) ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   seeks   to   renew   the   Agreement   with   Benecard   for   a   one-year  term (the   “ Term ” ) from January 1, 2019 through December 31, 2019 (the   “ Amendment ” ) ; and  WHEREAS,   the   monthly   guaranteed   rates   fo r   2019   are   the   same   identical   rates   as  charged   for   2018 ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Mayor   has   recused   himself   from   the   renewal   of   the   Agreement,  including the execution of any documents related to the renewal of the Agreement.  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESO LVED   BY   THE   TOWN SHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   shall   and   hereby   does   authorize   the  renewal of   the Agreement for the year 2019 ; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Business   Administrator   be   and   is   hereby   authorize d   to   execute   all  documents   necessary to effectuate   the Amendment ; and be it further  RESOLVED   that   this   Resolution   shall   be   published   and   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s  Office for reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  Kar en J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Susan McCartney , Council President  Municipal Clerk  Adopted:   December 11 , 2018  I hereby certify funds are available from:  Account No.  John Gross,   CFO

( “ Delta ” ) ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   seeks   to   renew   the   Agreement   with   Delta   for   a   one -year   term  (the   “ Term ” ) from January 1, 201 9   through December 31, 201 9   (the   “ Amendment ” ) ; and  WHEREAS,   the   monthly   guaranteed   rates   for   the   calendar   yea r   2019   are   16%   lower  than the rates   charged   for 2018;  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   shall   and   hereby   does   authorize   the  Amendment ; and b e it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Business   Admini strator   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   all  documents necessary to effectuate   the Amendment ; and be it further  RESOLVED   that   this   Resolution   shall   be   published   and   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s  Office   for reasonable inspection in accordance with ap plicable law. pages.text: 279 -18  December 11 , 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange ’ s   Insurance   Broker   solicited   offers   and  proposals for Stop Loss Coverage for the Township ’ s 201 8   Health Insurance   Coverage;   and  WHEREAS,   the   Township ’ s   broker   recommends   the   propos al   submitted   by   Sun   Life  Financial   ( “ Sun Life ” ), annexed hereto ; ”   and  WHEREAS ,   the   Mayor   has   recused   himself   from   the   award   of   this   Agreement,   including  the execution of any documents related to this Agreement.  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   T HE   TOWNSHIP   CO UNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   shall   and   hereby   does   authorize   the  Amendment, at the guaranteed rates set forth therein ; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Business   Administrator   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   exec ute   all  docume nts necessary to effectuate   the Amendment ; and be it further  RESOLVED   that   this   Resolution   shall   be   published   and   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s  Office for reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. Car nevale, R.M.C.   Susan McCartney , Council President  Municipal Clerk  Adopted:   December 11 , 2018  I hereby certify funds are available from:  Account No.  John Gross,   CFO
STOP - LOSS  Expertise  Renewal proposal created for  Township of West Orange  Presented by  Tom Nauta  Telephone: 917 - 716 - 4202  Thomas.Nauta@sunlife.com

Why choose Sun Life again?  Proposal for Township of West Orange  GSLOT-2551, SLPC 24342 0518 (exp. 520)  •   Dedicated and  knowledgeable stop -  loss experts  •   Clinical risk  management and cost -  containment programs  and cost - saving  opportunities  •   Customized  benchmarking for easier  decision - making  •   Over 35 years of stop -  loss expertise  •   A history of strong  financial ratings  •   Retain virtually all risk  and have full decision -  making authority  •   Commitment   to  delivering a ctionable  data and insights to  support the self - funded  community  •   Easy, hassle - free claims  reimbursement   process,  even for the largest  claims  •   Clear and  comprehensive contract  •   Mirroring of underlying  plan to avoid gaps in  coverage  •   Cash flow options for  faster reimbursement  •   Wide range of product  options, benefits, and  services  •   Tailored, flexible  coverage designed to  meet the needs of your  business  •   Predictable renewals  •   Innovative approaches  to educational  opportunities  SOLUTIONS   SPECIALIZATION  SEAMLESS EXPERIENCE   STRENGTH  EXPERTISE

Predictable renewals  How pooling helps deliver renewal rate stability over time  To help produce more consistent renewal costs, we combine your company’s Stop-Loss experience with that  of other customers. This is called “pooling”, which means that the overall cost of claims is shared by your  company and the others in the pool. It provides stability because Sun Life’s entire book of Stop-Loss  business is large enough to absorb the claim fluctuations that would be produced by any one customer.  The chart above shows how our pooled approach can provide protection against significant changes in renewal  costs. Your Stop-Loss claims can rise and fall from year to year, largely due to the volatility often associated with  catastrophic claims. In a non-pooled approach, your renewal premium would likely be adjusted each year to reflect  your individual claims experience. The corresponding rate changes might be extreme, depending on how poor or  how favorable the experience was during the most recent contract periods. Pooling decreases volatility, since the cost  of the catastrophic claim is shared by the entire customer pool. Over the last three   years, only 4% of renewal increases were greater than 40%, only 12% were  greater than 30% and only 23% exceeded 20%. Date:   January 1, 2019  Specific Stop-Loss coverage  The following options and programs are included in your policy:  Options:  •   No New Lasers at Renewal option with Renewal Rate Cap of 50%  This option prevents new lasers. The rate cap applies to Specific rates and the Aggregating Specific  deductible (if applicable), and it assumes there are no material changes to the underlying plan, the Sun Life  Stop-Loss policy, or the covered group.  •   Mirroring Endorsement  This option is subject to review and approval by Sun Life and may affect the quoted rates. To  include this endorsement with your policy, within 90 days of the policy effective date, we need  your plan document and an executed Renewal Options signature page.  •   Advance Funding option  This option enhances the cash flow of your self-funded plan by advancing the stop-loss funds to you  or your administrator up front, before you pay the provider.  •   Retiree coverage  Programs:  •   SunElite ℠   medical document review service  SunElite is a medical plan document review service for Sun Life Stop-Loss clients. Your custom SunElite  report will analyze the plan's cost-containment, federal law compliance and discretionary authority.  •   SunResources ®   preferred network program  SunResources is our preferred cost-containment vendor program. On a voluntary basis, Sun Life clients  gain access to an extensive network of cost-containment vendors that have gone through a rigorous  vetting process to ensure quality service and performance. SunResources can help lower costs both  before and after a catastrophic claim occurs.  The following are not included in your policy:  •   Clinical Trials option  •   Electronic Funds Transfer  •   Terminal Liability option  •   Experience Rating Refund  Assumptions  •   Transplant rider  Sun Life assumes that if a Transplant rider is in place with your health-care plan, Sun Life is  designated as secondary claims payer.  Producer commissions  Sun Life pays the following commission percent to the Stop-Loss producer: 0.0%.  Specific Stop-Loss renewal acceptance  Acceptance of your Specific Stop-Loss renewal by Sun Life is subject to timely receipt of a signed renewal  proposal and contingent upon a review of large claims over $50,000 with diagnosis/prognosis for the period of  January 1, 2018, through September 30, 2018, with accompanying required information. For large claims,  the required information includes paid claims, pending claims, and notification of known situations. Upon  review of your large claims information, we reserve the right to recalculate quoted rates.  October 15, 2018

Upon review of that information, we reserve the  right to recalculate the Aggregate Attachment Point.  General coverage  •   Assumptions  The above rates assume that your underlying plan will be brought into compliance with the Mental Health  Parity Act and that covered expenses, as defined under the Mental Health Parity Act, will be covered as  "any other illness". This renewal proposal also assumes that there are no underlying plan limits that are  inconsistent with the guidelines established by the Americans with Disabilities Act.  •   Affordable Care Act accommodations  This renewal proposal represents Sun Life's efforts to work with you to meet your requirements under the  Affordable Care Act (ACA), including, but not limited to, the dependent age provisions of the ACA. According  to the rescission provision of the ACA, the self-funded medical plan is responsible for keeping its census  data up to date at all times. If the plan inadvertently does not remove a terminated participant, Sun Life  may deny any claims from the participant. However, in that situation, the self-funded medical plan is  responsible for the claim. documents.title: AUTHORIZING EXECUTION OF GRANT AGREEMENT WITH THE LEARY FIREFIGHTERS FOUNDATION AS TO FREE - STANDING ROOF PROP AND MATERIALS

pages.text: 280-18  December 11, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Leary   Firefighters   Foundation   (the   “ Foundation ” )   is   a   non-profit  organization   that   provides   funding   and   equipment   to   fire   departments   throughout   the   United  States; and  WHEREAS,   the   Foundation   has   awarded   to   the   West   Orange   Fire   Dep artment   (the   “ Fire  Department ” )   a   grant   for   the   purchase   of   a   Free-Standing   Roof   Prop   and   Materials   (the  “ Equipment ” )   with an estimated cost of $24,979; and  WHEREAS,   the   Fire   Department   intends   to   install   the   Equipment   outside   of   Fire   Station  3   or   may   inst all   the   equi pment   at   the   Fire   Department   Headquarters   for   training   of   its  firefighters; and  WHEREAS,   the   Foundation   requires   that   the   Township   of   West   Orange   enter   into   a  Grant Agreement, annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A ”   (the   “ Agreement ” ); and  WHEREAS,   the   Ag reement   prov ides   that   the   Foundation   will   purchase   the   Equipment  for   the   Fire   Department   and   that   the   Township   will   be   responsible   for   maintaining   and   repair   the  Equipment as needed.  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WE ST   ORANGE   th at   the   Mayor   and/or   Township   Business   Administrator   be  and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the   Agreement,   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A ”   on   behalf   of  the   Township   and   the   Municipal   Clerk   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   attest   to   the   signature (s)   of  the Mayor   and/or   the   Township Business Administrator .  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: Dece mber 11, 2018  4827-3287 -1298, v. 1
Exhibit   “A”
DM2 \ 7668167.2  THE LEARY FIREFIGHTERS FOUNDATION  GRANT AGREEMENT

DM2 \ 7668167.2  THE LEARY FIREFIGHTERS FOUNDATION  GRANT AGREEMENT  This Grant Agreement (the “Agreement”) is made and entered into on ______ __, 20__  (the “Execution Date”) by and among The Leary Firefighters Foundation, a nonprofit  corporation (“Foundation”), and   Township of West Orange Fire Department ,   a paid fire  depar tment   (“Grantee”) or, with reference to the following:  I.   RECITALS  1.   The Foundation is a nonprofit organization described in Section 501(c)(3) of the  Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended (the “Code”).  2.   Grantee is a fire department providing fire, emergency   and rescue services in  West Orange, New Jersey , to which contributions are deductible under Code Section 170(c)(1).  3.   The Foundation desires to purchase, on behalf of Grantee, a   Free - Standing Roof  Prop and Materials   at a cost of approximately   Twenty - Four Tho usand Nine Hundred and  Seventy - Nine   Dollars ($ 24,979.00 ).  NOW THEREFORE, the Foundation and Grantee hereby agree as follows:  II.   TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE AGREEMENT  The terms and conditions of this Agreement are as follows:  1.   Grant .   Subject to satisfaction o f the terms, conditions and all other requirements  set forth in this Agreement, the Foundation hereby pledges to purchase on behalf of and for the  benefit of Grantee the following equipment (the “Equipment”):   a Free - Standing Roof Prop and  Materials   (the “G rant Purpose”) at the estimated cost of   Twenty - Four Thousand Nine Hundred  and Seventy - Nine Dollars ($24,979.00)   (the “Grant”).   In the event the actual cost of the  Equipment exceeds the Grant amount by more than five percent (5%), the Foundation may, in it s  sole and absolute discretion, terminate this Agreement and have no further obligation whatsoever  to Grantee. The Grant shall only be used for the Grant Purpose, and no amount of the Grant may  be applied for any other use or purpose without the prior wri tten consent of the Foundation, in its  sole and absolute discretion.   Such Grant shall be paid directly to the vendor of the Equipment.  Whenever possible, upon purchase of the Equipment, title to the Equipment shall be vested directly  in the name of Grant ee.  2.   Termination of Grant . Upon the occurrence of any one of the events listed below  (a “Grant Termination Event”), the Foundation shall not be obligated to make any further payments  of the Grant or purchase of the Equipment, and (ii) the Foundation may d emand that Grantee return  the Equipment to the Foundation: The Grant Termination Events are as follows:  (a)   Grant funds are applied for purposes not permitted under Section   3 of this  Agreement.

2  DM2 \ 7668167.2  (b)   Grantee is in material breach of any provision of this Agreement.  (c)   Grantee ceases to be described in Code Section 170(c)(1).  3.   Sole Purpose of Grant . All parties hereto understand and agree that the Grant shall  only be used for the Grant Purpose as contemplated by this Agreement. Use of any portion of the  Grant for any   other purposes must have the prior written approval of the Foundation.   Without  limiting the foregoing, Grantee agrees that the Grant will not be used for any of the following  purposes: (i) to attempt to influence legislation or attempt to influence the ou tcome of any specific  election, including conducting, directly or indirectly, any voter registration drive; (ii) to make  grants to individuals or to other organizations; or (iii) to undertake any activity other than for  charitable purposes within the meani ng of Code Section   l70(c)(2)(B).  4.   Maintenance of Equipment.   Grantee shall maintain the Equipment in good working  order over its useful life in accordance with the manufacturer recommendations. In the event the  Equipment becomes defective during the appl icable warranty period, Grantee shall (i) promptly  seek repair or replacement of the Equipment from the manufacturer or vendor, and (ii) Grant ee  shall notify the Foundation of such defect and the resolution of the defect.  5.   Nonliability; Indemnification .   Grantee agrees that the Foundation shall have no  liability whatsoever for any defect or malfunction in the Equipment or for any direct or indirect  loss, damage, claim or cost arising therefrom.   Grantee shall indemnify and hold Foundation  harmless from   and against any claim, loss, liability or damage (including attorneys’ fees) arising  out of, or in connection with, the Equipment and/or the use of the Equipment by Grantee or any  other person.  6.   Financial Report and Updates .   Within ninety   (90) days afte r date on which the  Grant is provided to Grantee, Grantee shall provide the Foundation with a report on the deployment  of the Equipment. Grantee shall provide the Foundation a full report on the use of the Equipment  within twelve (12) months of the date o f receipt of the Grant and for five (5) years annually  thereafter. The reports shall include an update on how the Grant was used to better serve the  community as well as any challenges faced. The reports must be signed by the “chief” of the  Grantee. Grant ee agrees to cooperate and respond to periodic inquiries from the Foundation in its  efforts to monitor the use of the Grant.  7.   Use of Names and Likeness. . Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained  herein, Grantee shall not use, publish, disseminat e, display or refer in any way to the name,  likeness, image or biography of Denis Leary or the name of The Leary Firefighters Foundation or  any entity related to Denis Leary or The Leary Firefighters Foundation, including, without  limitation, in any contri butor lists, solicitation materials, promotional materials, news materials,  publicity materials, social media, or commemorative materials created, published or used by  Grantee, without express prior written consent of the Foundation, in its sole and absolu te  discretion.

3  DM2 \ 7668167.2  III.   GENERAL PROVISIONS  1.   Cumulative Remedies . Notwithstanding any rights or remedies which are set forth  in this Agreement, such rights or remedies shall not be exclusive and the parties hereto shall have  any and all rights or remedies availab le at law or in equity.  2.   Additional Documents . Provided the other party is not in default, each party agrees  to execute all other instruments, documents or other papers, and perform all other acts necessary  and proper to carry out and consummate the trans actions contemplated hereby.  3.   New York Law Governs .   This Agreement in all respects shall be subject to the  laws of the State of New York.  4.   Severability .   A determination by a court of competent jurisdiction that any  provision of this Agreement or any part   thereof is illegal or unenforceable shall not cancel or  invalidate the remainder of such provision or this Agreement, which shall remain in full force and  effect.  5.   Valid Existence . Each entity executing this Agreement represents and warrants that  such en tity is duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of the  jurisdiction in which it is formed.  6.   Authority . Each person executing this Agreement hereby warrants and represents  that he or she is duly authorized and empowered by t he entity on whose behalf he or she is signing  this Agreement to execute and deliver this Agreement as the agreement of said entity and that this  Agreement is binding upon such entity in accordance with its terms.  7.   Notices . All notices and writings requir ed or permitted under this Agreement shall  be mailed, delivered or transmitted by one party to the other party at its notice address provided  on the signature pages of this Agreement, and each such notice shall be deemed effective and  given:   upon receipt   if personally delivered; upon receipt if sent by telegram, telex, telecopy or  electronic mail; upon receipt or upon the date of first attempted delivery, if sent by certified or  registered mail with postage prepaid, return receipt requested, or if sent by   Federal Express or  other nationally recognized carrier service; or upon receipt if sent in any other way.   Any party  hereto may from time to time, by written notice to the other, designate a different notice address  which shall be substituted for the one s pecified on the signature pages of this Agreement.  8.   Counterparts .   This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which  shall be deemed to be a duplicate original. All parties to this Agreement agree that they will each  execute a duplicate origin al of the Agreement as soon as possible upon receipt, and that a copy of  each original signature page will be sent via facsimile or electronic transmission to each party  immediately after execution.  9.   Amendments .   This Agreement may be amended only by a wri tten instrument  signed by each of the parties hereto.  10.   Waivers . The waiver by one party of the performance of any covenant, condition,  or promise shall not invalidate this Agreement, nor shall it be considered as a waiver by such party

4  DM2 \ 7668167.2  of any other covena nt, condition, or promise.   The delay in pursuing any remedy or in insisting  upon full performance for any breach or failure of any covenant, condition, or promise shall not  prevent a party from later pursuing any remedies or insisting upon full performanc e for the same  or any similar breach or failure.  11.   Recitals .   The Recitals are hereby incorporated into and made part of this  Agreement.  12.   Attorneys Fees; Forum . In the event of any litigation to enforce the provisions of  this Agreement, the prevailing party   in such litigation shall be entitled to reasonable attorneys’  fees as fixed by the court. Any litigation to enforce or interpret the provisions of this Agreement  or the parties’ rights and liabilities arising out of this Agreement or the performance here under  shall be maintained only in the courts located in New York, New York.  IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed and delivered this Agreement as of  the date set forth above. documents.title: REVISED RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING SUBORDINATION OF MORTGAGE RE 4 PILLOT STREET FOLLOWING REQUEST FOR ADVISORS MORTGAGE

pages.text: 281-18  December 11, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Housing   Rehabilitation   Program   has   a  mortgage   against   certain   real   property   owned   by   John   Stein   and   Sandra   Stein   (the   “ Owners ” )  residing   at   4   Pillot   Place ,   Block   24,   Lot   49   (the   “ Property ” ),   in   the   amount   of   Thirty-Five  Thousand   Dollars   ($35,000.00),   which   was   dated   January   11,   2013   and   recorded   in   the   Essex  County   Register ’ s   Office   on   March   26,   2013   in   Book   14242,   Page   9059   (the   “ WOHRP  Mortgage ” ); and  WHEREAS,   on   November   27,   2018,   the   Township   Council   adop ted   Resolution   263-18 ,  annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A, ”   authorizing   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   to  execute   a   Subordination   Agreement   allowing   for   the   subordination   of   the   WOHRP   Mortgage  allowing   the   Owners   to   obtain   a   Reverse   Mortgage   from   A dvisors   Mortgage   Group,   LLC  ( “ Advisors   Mortgage ” )   on   the   Property   and   avoid   a   Sheriff ’ s   sale   of   the   Property   following   a  foreclosure action brought by   the lender currently holding a mortgage on the Property; and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   Resolution   263-18,   the   Mayor   executed   the   proposed  Subordination Agreement; and  WHEREAS,   Advisors   Mortgage   has   notified   the   Township   that   the   amount   of   the   reverse  mortgage   was   incorrect   and   must   be   corrected   pursuant   to   a   proposed   Amended   Subordination  Agreement, annexed heret o as Exhibit   “ B; ”   and  WHEREAS ,   the   Amended   Subordination   Agreement   provides   that   the   maximum   claim  amount   for   the   Reverse   Mortgage   at   $442,500   would   exceed   the   appraised   value   of   the  Property at $295,000; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township,   nevertheless,   desires   to   allow   the   owners   to   avoid  foreclosure on the Property.  NOW   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Mayor   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   execute   the

2  Amended   Subordination   Agreement ,   annexed   hereto   as   Exhib it   “ B , ”   in   favor   of   Advisors  Mortgage   with respect to the Property and the Owner s ; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Municipal   Clerk   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   attest   to   the  Mayor ’ s signature on the Subordination of Mortgage ; and be it further  RES OLVED,   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office  in accordance with applicable law. Voorhees Street, Suite C, Danville, IL 61834 (collectively referred to as "Advisors Mortgage  Group").  WHEREAS   the Lienholder is the holder of a mortgage in the principal amount of $ 35,000.00   executed by   John S.  and Sandra M. Stein , (the "Borrowers"), dated   01/11/2013   and recorded on   03/26/2013 in Book 12424, at page  9059 , in the Essex County Register's Office, covering the property commonly known as   4 Pillot Place, West Orange,  NJ 07052   (the "Property") and legally described as   Block 24, Lot 49 .  WHEREAS   Advisors Mortgage Group intends to make a Reverse Mortgage Loan (First Position) to the Borrower  with a maximum claim amount not to exceed $442,500.00 and a face amount of   $442,500.00 , and date on our  about   , 2018 to be secured by a mortgage to MERS as nominee for Advisors Group, LLC covering the  Property (the “Advisors Mortgage Group Lien”), together with a mortgage made by the Secretary of Housing and  Urban Development (the “HUD Reverse Lien”) to the Borrowers with a maximum claim amount not to exceed  $442,500.00   and a face amount of   $442,500.00 , and dated on our about   , 2018, to be recorded  simultaneously therewith.  WHEREAS   Advisors Mortgage Group will only make the loan to the Borrowers provided that the Lienholder's lien is  subordinate to both the Advisors Mortgage Group Lien and the HUD Reverse Lien.  WHEREAS   Lienholder intends that Advisors Mortgage Group's Lien and HUD Reverse Lien both be prior and  superior to the Lienholder's lien (“Third Lien Position”), and  NOW THEREFORE , it is agreed that in consideration of one dollar and other good and valuable consideration, the  adequacy and receipt of which is hereby acknowledged, Lienholder agrees to subordinate and make Lienholder's  lien subordinate and junior in all respects to the Advisors Mortgage Group Lien and HUD Reverse Lien, said  Lienholder's lien being in Third Lien Position.

pages.text: 282-18  December 11, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   issued   a   Request   for   Bid   Proposal  for   Janitorial   Services   (the   “ RFP ” )   at   several   municipal   buildings   including   West   Orange   Town   Hall,  West   Orange   Police   Building,   West   Orange   Senior   Health   Center,   Dep artment   of   Public  Works/Engineering,   Department   of   Recreation,   West   Orange   Police   Valley   Road,   and   West   Orange  Police Washington Street Sub Station ;   and  WHEREAS,   on   October 30 , 201 8 ,   the   Township received   eight (8)   bids in response to the R F P:  (1)   Ocean   C lean   from   Cedar   Grove,   New   Jersey   with   a   bid   price   of  $ 42 , 884   per   year,   with   additional   optional   hourly   rates   of   $35   per   hour  for buffing floors and $85 per hour for shampooing carpeting;  (2)   Excell ent   Building   Services   Corp.   from   Orange ,   New   Jersey  ( “ Ex cellent   Building ” )   with   a   bid   price   of   $ 44,376   per   year,   with  additional   optional   hourly   rates   of   $ 35   per   hour   for   buffing   floors   and  $ 35   per hour for shampooing carpeting ;  ( 3 )   Cleaning   Services   of   Hudson   Valley,   Inc.   from   Garnerville ,   New  York   with   a   bid   price   of   $ 47 , 333.13   per   year,   with   additional   optional  hourly   rates   of   $ 18   per   hour   for   buffing   floors   and   $ 18   per   hour   for  shampooing carpeting ;  ( 4 )   Best   Building   Services,   Inc.   from   Ridgefield ,   New   Jersey   with   a   bid  price   of   $ 47 , 400   per   year ,   with   addi tional   optional   hourly   rates   of   $ 24  per hour for buffing floors and $ 24   per hour for shampooing carpeting ;  ( 5 )   Tropical   Cleaning   Service,   LLC   from   Kearney ,   New   Jersey   with   a  bid   price   of   $ 54 , 883   per   year,   with   additional   optional   hourly   rates   o f  $ 40   per   h our   for   buffing   floors   and   $ 35   per   hour   for   shampooing  carpeting ;  ( 6 )   Community   Institute   for   Career   Advancement   from   East   Orange ,  New   Jersey   with   a   bid   price   of   $ 120 , 500   per   year,   with   additional  optional   hourly   rates   of   $ 32   per   hour   for   buffi ng   floors   a nd   $ 38   per   hour  for shampooing carpeting ;  ( 7 )   Quality   Facility   Solutions   Corp.   from   Brooklyn,   New   York   with   a   bid  price   of   $ 157 , 410.05   per   year,   with   additional   optional   hourly   rates   of  $ 0.28   per   hour   for   buffing   floors   and   $ 0.17   per   hour   for   sh ampooing  ca rpeting ; and  ( 8 )   T.U.C.S.   Cleaning   Services,   Inc.   from   Orange ,   New   Jersey   with   a   bid  price   of   $ 282,024   per   year,   with   additional   optional   hourly   rates   of   $ 3 0  per hour for buffing floors and $ 30   per hour for shampooing carpeting ;  and

WHEREAS,   O cean   Clean   advised   that   Township   that   it   would   not   enter   into   an   Agreement   with  the Township, thereby defaulting under its bid; and  WHEREAS,   the   bid   from   Excellent   Building   submitted   the   remaining   lowest   bid   for   the   Contract  for Janitorial Services (the   “ A greement ” ),   annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A ; ”  WHEREAS,   the   term   of   the   Agreement   shall   be   for   a   term   of   two   (2)   years   with   an   option   to  extend the   Agreement   for one additional year .  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   O RANGE   that   the   Township   shall   award   the   Agreement   to   Excellent   Building  consistent   with   the   terms   and   conditions   provided   in   the   Agreement ,   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A; ”   and  it is further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   t he   Agreemen t   on   behalf   of  the   Township   and   the   Municipal   Clerk   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   attest   to   the   Mayor ’ s   signature;   and  it is further  RESOLVED,   that   the   original   of   the   Agreement   between   the   Township   and   Excellent   Building  shall be maintaine d in the Mu nicipal Clerk ’ s office.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   December 11, 2018  I hereby certify funds are available from:  Account No.  John Gross  C hief Financial Officer  4833-2059-5330, v. 1

AGREEMENT FOR JANITORIAL SERVICES  THIS AGREEMENT   made and entered into on this ____ day of December, 2018 by  and between the   TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE , a municipal corporation of the State of  New Jersey, located at Town Hall, 66 Main Street, West Orange, County of Essex and State of  New Jersey, hereinafter called the   “TOWNSHIP” , party of the first part, and   EXCELLENT  BUILDING SERVICES CORP. , a New Jersey corporation with offices at 70 South Jefferson  Street, Orange, New Jersey 07050, hereinafter referred to as   “EXCELLENT BUILDING” ,  party of the second part.  WITNESSETH THAT:  WHEREAS,   the   Township   issued   a   Request   for   Bid   Proposal,   (the  “SPECIFICATIONS” )   seeking to award a Contract   for Janitorial Services   at   seven (7)  buildings owned and operated by the   TOWNSHIP;   and  WHEREAS , the Specifications set forth the terms and conditions of the Janitorial  Services to be performed under this agreement; and  WHEREAS , on October 30, 2018,   EXCELLENT BUILDING   submitted a bid in  response to   SPECIFICATION   (the   “BID” ), annexed hereto as Exhibit   “A;”   and  WHEREAS , the   BID   included and incorporated the   SPECIFICATIONS ; and  WHEREAS , the   BID   set forth an annual base bid of $44,376 per year with additional  optional rates for buffing floors at $35 per hour and shampooing carpeting at $35 per hour; and  NOW, THEREFORE,   in consideration of the mutual promises, terms and conditions  hereinafter set forth, the parties hereto agree as follows:  1.   EXCELLENT BUILDING   hereby agrees to provide the services and other  covenants set forth in the   BID , annexed hereto as Exhibit   “A.”

2  2.   As set forth in the   SPECIFICATIONS,   the term of this Agreement shall be for  two (2) years starting on January 1, 2019 and that the Township shall have an option to renew  this Agreement for additional one year.  3.   The   TOWNSHIP   and   EXCELLENT BUILDING   hereby agree to the rates set  forth in the   BID   and to the payment process set forth in Section M of the   SPECIFICATIONS.  4.   The   TOWNSHIP   and   EXCELLENT BUILDING   hereby agree that to the  extent that the   BID   conflicts with the terms and conditions of the   SPECIFICATIONS , the  SPECIFICATIONS   shall govern.  IN WITNESS WHEREOF,   the parties have set their hands and seals the day and year  first above written.  ATTEST:  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, R.M.C  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  By:  ROBERT D. PARISI, MAYOR  WITNESS:   EXCELLENT BUILDING SERVICES  CORP.  By:
Exhibit   “A”

documents.title: RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING SECOND TEMPORARY EXTENSION OF CONTRACTO TO RELIABLE WOOD FOR OPERATION OF RECYCLING CENTER

pages.text: 284-18  December 11, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   is   the   owner   of   real  property   known   as   Block   170,   Lots   13,   15.03   and   17   on   the   Tax   Map   of   the   Township  commonly known as 590 Mt. Pleasant Avenue   (the   “ Property ” ) ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   ut ilizes   the   P roperty   as   an   integral   part   of   its   Recycling  Program; and  WHEREAS ,   the   operations   conducted   at   the   Property   include   the   composting   of  leaves,   the   processing   of   vegetative   wood   waste   and   operating   a   recycling   convenience  center   for   West   Orange   residents   for   mixed   paper,   cardboard,   consumer   electronics,   tires,  clean wood , and commingled containers; and  WHEREAS ,   in   2013,   the   Township   entered   into   an   agreement   with   Reliable   Wood  Products,   LLC   ( “ Reliable   Wood ” )   of   393   Lincoln   Boulevard ,   Middlesex ,   N ew   Jersey,  0 8846   for   the   operation   of   the   Recycling   Center   for   Class   B   materials,   exempt   wood,  Class   C   materials,   exempt   compost   and   to   operate   the   Recycling   Center   for   the   collection  and   processing   of   mixed   paper,   cardboard   and   commingled   containers   (the   “ Operations ” )  for   a   period   of   three   (3)   years   with   two   (2)   additional   one   year   options,   annexed   hereto   as  Exhibit   “ A ”   (the   “ Agreement ” ) ; and  WHEREAS ,   the Township authorized the two (2) additional one-year options;  WHEREAS ,   on   June   9,   2018,   the   Township   ad vertised   a   Request   for   Proposal  ( “ RFP ” )   entitled   “ Request   for   Proposal   for   Operation   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange  Recycling   Center ”   to   award   a   new   Contract   to   operate   the   West   Orange   Recycling   Center  (the   “ Contract ” ) following the expiration of the Agree ment ; and  WHEREAS ,   on   July   17,   2018,   the   Township   received   a   single   proposal   from  Reliable Wood (the   “ Proposal ” ), and  WHEREAS ,   the   Proposal   contains   certain   exceptions   and   conditions   to   be  examined   and   evaluated   by   the   Township   prior   to   a   determination   as   to   the   award   of   the  Contract , and  WHEREAS ,   on   or   about   September   4,   2018,   the   Township   Council   adopted  Resolution   206-18   which   authorized   the   Township   to   enter   into   an   Temporary   Extension  of   the   Contract   until   December   31,   2018   to   explore   revisions   to   th e   RFP   and   the

Operations described therein, and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   still   requires   addition   time   to   explore   possible  revisions to the RFP and the Operations   at the Property , and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   requires   additional   time   to   determine   the   award   of   t he  Contract,   but still requires   the Operations at the Property; and  WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:11-24(a),   Reliable   Wood   consented   to   an  additional   extensio n of its proposal until January   31, 201 9 ; and  WHEREAS ,   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A ”   is   a   copy   of   a   proposed   second  agreement   (the   “ Temporary   Second   Agreement ” )   authorizing   a   second   temporary  extension   of   the   Agreement   until   January   31,   201 9   to   allow   the   Township   to   evaluate   and  determine the award of the Contract   or whether revisions are required .  NOW,   THEREFORE   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP  COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE   that   the   Township   is   authorized   to  enter into the Temporary   Second   Agreement, annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A; ”   an be it further  RESOLVED   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   her eby   authorized   to   execute   the   Temporary  Second   Agreement,   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A ”   and   the   Township   Clerk   be   and   is   hereby  authorized to attest to the Mayor ’ s Signature.  Karen J. Carnevale , R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  Municipal Clerk   C ouncil President  Adopted:   December 11, 2018  4834-3557-4658, v. 1

2557-18  AN ORDINANCE TO VACATE, RELEASE AND EXTINGUISH THE PUBLIC RIGHT  TO A PORTION OF ELMWOOD AVENUE   IN THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  FROM ROOSEVELT AVENUE TO GARFIELD AVENUE  BE IT ORDAINED   by the Townsh i p Council of the Township of West Orange and   County of Essex,  State of New Jersey, as follows:  SECTION I:   That portion of Elmwood Avenue from Roosevelt Avenue to Garfield   Avenue, as  hereinafter described, is hereby vacated, and any and all public rights therein are hereby released and  extinguished.  Beginning at the intersection of the northerly sideline of Elmwood Avenue and the   westerly  sideline of Roosevelt Avenue , 50 feet   wide ,   alongside Block 172.10, Lot 2,   as shown on the Tax Map s   of  the Town ship of West Orange, thence:  1.   North 63 degrees 23 min utes 00 seconds   W est ,   280   feet to the easterly sideline of Garfield  Avenue, thence  2. South 63   degrees 23 minutes 00 seconds East ,   280 feet to the wester ly l ine of Roosevelt Avenue,  thence  4.   North 26 degrees   37 minutes 00 seconds,   50   feet   to the point and place of beginning .  Containing 14 , 000 square feet .  SECTION II:   The vacated portions of Elmwood Avenue   shall revert and be annexed to the  adjoining parcels as   described below.  A.   Beginning at the   intersection of the   northerly sideline of Elmwood Avenue and the westerly  sid eline of Roosevelt Avenue, thence:  1.   North 63 degrees 23   minutes 00 seconds   W est ,   140   feet , then  2.   South 26 degrees 37minutes 00 seconds   W est ,   25   feet , thence  3.   South   63 degrees 23   minutes   00   seconds   E ast ,   14 0   feet , thence  4.   North 26 degrees 37 minutes 00 seconds   East,   25   feet   to the point and place of beginning .

Containing 3,500 square feet.  To revert and be annexed to Block 172.10, Lot 2 as   shown on the Tax Maps of the Township of West  Orange and commonly known as 100 Roosevelt Avenue.  B.   Beginning at   the intersection   of the southerly   sideline   of   Elmwood Avenue and the westerly  sideline of Roosevelt Avenue ,   thence :  1.   North 63 degrees 23   minutes 0   seconds   West,   140   feet , thence  2.   North 26 degrees 37   minutes   00   seconds East ,   25 feet,   thence  3.   South 63 degrees 23   minutes 00 seconds   East ,   140   feet , thence  4.   South 26 degrees 37   minutes 00 seconds   West ,   25   feet   to the point and place of beginning .  Containing 3 ,500 square feet.  To revert and be annexed to Block 172.05, Lot 8 as shown on the Tax Maps of the Township of West  Orange and commonly known as 102 Roosevelt Avenue.  SECTION III.   A 10 foot wide drainage easement shall be established within the   vacated  portions of Elmwood Avenue that revert to Block 172.10, Lot 2 and Block 172.05, Lot 8 as follows:  A.   Beginning at the   intersection of the   new dividing line between   Lot 2 in Block 172.10 and Lot  8 in B lock 172.05   and   the westerly sideline of Elmwood Av enue   and   proceeding:  1.   W est   along the dividing line   one hundred and forty (140) feet; thence  2.   North   on a perpendicular to the dividing line   ten (10) feet; thence  3.   East   and   parallel with the dividing line   one hundred forty (140) feet to the extended  westerly   line of Elmwood Avenue; thence  4.   South   along the westerly line of Elmwood Avenue   ten (10) feet to the point and place of  beginning.  Being a ten (10) foo t drainage and utility easement .  Containing 1,400 square feet within Block 172.10, Lot 2 commonly   known as 100 Roosevelt Ave.  B.   Beginning at the   intersection of the   new dividing line between Lot 2 in Block 172.10 and Lot  8 in Block 172.05   and   the westerly sideline of Elmwood Avenue and proceeding :  1.   W est along the dividing line one hundred and forty (140)   feet; thence

2.   South on a perpendicular to the dividing line ten (10) feet; thence  3.   East   and   parallel with the dividing line one hundred forty (140) feet to the extended  westerly line of Elmwood Avenue; thence  4.   North along the westerly line of Elmwood Avenu e ten (10) feet to the point and place of  beginning.  Being a ten (10) foot drainage and utility easement .  Containing 1,400 square feet within Block 172.05, Lot 8 commonly known as 102 Roosevelt Ave.  SECTION IV.   It is the purpose of this Ordinance to aban don, release and extinguish the public  rights, including any right to build a public roadway, in Elmwood Avenue between Roosevelt Avenue and  Garfield Avenue. The northerly half of the vacated right of way alongside Block 172.10, Lot 2 shall revert  and be   annexed to that lot subject to a 10 foot wide drainage and utility easement along the new  southerly l ine of that lot. The southerly   half of the vacated right of way alongside Block 172.05, Lot 8  shall revert and be annexed to that lot subject to a 10 foot   wide drainage and utility easement along the  new northerly line of that lot.  SECTION V.   The Township Clerk shall, within sixty (60) days after the effective date of this  Ordinance, file a copy of the wi thin Ordinance, certified by her   under the seal of t he Township of West  Orange to be a true copy, together with Proof of Pu blication in the office of the R egister of Deeds of the  County of Essex, all in accordance with N.J.S.A. 40:67-21.  SECTIO N VI .   Any and all ordinances or parts of ordinance inconsistent   with the provisions of  this ordinance are hereby repealed to the extent of such inconsistency.  SECTION VII .   This ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and after publication in  accordance with law.  K A REN   J.   CARNEVA LE,   R. M.C.   ROBERT PARISI , MAYOR  Intr od uced: November 27, 2018   SUSAN McCARTNEY, COUNCIL PRESIDENT  Adopted: December 11, 2018

2557-18  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance vacates Elmwood Avenue between Roosevelt Avenue and Garfield Avenue which is a  paper street . The vacation of the right of way releases and extinguishes any and all public rights to this  portion of Elmwood Aven ue including barring the right to build a public road within the vacated right of  way. Although this portion of Elmwood Avenue will be vacated, access to property owned by the  Township of West Orange that could previously be accessed through Elmwood Avenu e may still be  accessed through other public rights of way including Garfield Avenue and Mt. Vernon Avenue. The  vacated section of the Elmwood Avenue right of way between Roosevelt Avenue and Garfield Avenue  will revert to the adjoining property owners wh ich includes the Township of West Orange and the  owners of Block 172.10, Lot 2 and Block 172.05, Lot 8 commonly known as 100 Roosevelt Avenue and  102 Roosevelt Avenue respectively. Since a stream currently exists in a portion of vacated Elmwood  Avenue a 2 0 foot wide easement will be created around the centerline of the vacated right of way for  drainage and future utility purposes.

259-18  November 27, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   ( “ Township ” )   and   Borough   of  Roseland   ( “ Roseland ” )   have   previously   executed   Inter-Local   Agreements   whereby   the  Township   would   provide   Roseland   with   certain   animal   control   services   in   exchange   for  quarterly p ayments by Roseland to the Township (the   “ Inter-Local Agreement ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   now   intends   to   renew   the   Inter-Local   Agreement,   for   a  two   (2)-year   term,   whereby   animal   control   services   shall   be   provided   to   Roseland   from  July 1, 201 8   to June 3 0, 20 20 ; (the   “ Agreement ” ); and  WHEREAS,   under   the   Agreement,   the   Borough   of   Roseland   will   pay   the  Township   of   West   Orange   the   sum   of   $ 1 4,121.80   per   annum   to   be   prorated   on   a  quarterly   basis   of   $ 3, 530.45   per   quarter   for   the   contract   year   201 8   –   20 19   and   t he   sum  of   $ 1 4,827.91   per   annum   to   be   prorated   on   a   quarterly   basis   of   $3, 706.98   per   quarter  for the contract year 201 9   –   20 20 ; and  WHEREAS,   this   Resolution   authorizes   that   twenty-five   (25%)   of   the   per   annum  amount   for   each   contract   year   be   dedicated   to   an d   placed   in   the   dog   license   account  04-2010-00-6690-304   to   cover   the   cost   of   personnel   and   other   expenses   authorized  under the   agreement;   and  WHEREAS,   the   Township ’ s   Health   Officer   has   reviewed   the   Agreement   and  recommends execution of the terms containe d therein; and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   the   Roseland   Resolution   and   as   stated   in   the   Inter-Local  Agreement,   Roseland   has   agreed   to   indemnify   the   Township   for   any   services   provided  for the Borough, as stated in Schedule A attached to the Inter-Local Agreement;

NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   and   counter-sign  the   Inter-local   Agreement   with   the   Borough   of   Roseland   annexed   hereto   as   Attachment  “ A ” ,   and   the   Mu nicipal   Clerk   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   attest   to   the   Mayor ’ s  signature thereon.  Karen   J.   Carnevale , R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   Novembe r 27 , 2018  4832-4017-0778, v. 1

2 60-18  November 14, 2018  Mayor and Township Council  Municipal Building  66 Main Street  West Orange, New Jersey 07052  RE:   AWARD OF CONTRACT  SNOW PLOWING AND SALTING EQUIPMENT  FOR PLOWING, LOADING AND HAULING  Mayor Par isi, Council   President McCartney, and Council Members :  In October 2018 , bids were advertised for Snow Plowing and Salting and Equipment for Plowing,  Loadi ng and Hauling snow for the 2018   –   2019   season with the option to extend any contract for two  additional one year pe riods. On Tuesday, November 6, 2018 the bids were received.   Two included bids  for   snow plowing and salting. One of these two bids, from Abraham General Construction of West  Orang e was deemed unresponsive due to them not including a Bid Bond for the work as specified.  Another bid from American Asphalt and Milling Services, LLC of Kearny, New Jersey was responsive.  The snow plowing and salting portion of the bid documents requeste d ho urly rates to plow and  salt six   different routes in the Township.   The summary of the bids is attached.  I am only recommendi ng contracts be awarded for four of the six routes, as follows:   Routes 1A-  1 Valley, 1B-1 Heights, 1C-1 Merklin, and 1K-1 Lowe r Gregory are to be awarded to American Asphalt  & Milling Services, 96 Midland Avenue, Kearny, NJ 07032.  Additionally, I am recommending contracts be awarded to American Asphalt and Milling  Services, LLC for supplying equipment for loading and hauling sn ow.  4BB- 30 cubic yard Rolloffs, 4BC- Tandem Axle Trucks, 4BD- Tri-Axle Trucks, and  4BE- Front End Loaders.  Route 1F-1 Upper Gregory , and 1J-1 Stanford,   will not be awarded and will   be rebid.  A Resolution of Award is attached, and if you have any q uestions, please feel free to contact me.  Very truly yours,  Leonard R. Lepore, Municipal Engineer  Director of Public Works  LRL/  enc.  cc:   John K. Sayers  John Gross

262-18  November 2 7 , 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   on   June   9,   2015,   the   Township   Council   for   the   Township   of   West   Orange  (the   “ Township ” )   passed   Resolution   156-15   which   authorized   the   commencement   of   a  declaratory   judgment   action   consistent   with   the   transitiona l   process   established   in   the   New  Jersey   Supreme   Court   in   In   the   Matter   of   the   Adoption   of   N.J.A.C.   5:96   and   5:97   by   the   New  Jersey   Council   on   Affordable   Housing ,   221   N.J.   1   (2015)   (the   “ Affordable   Housing   Decision ” )   to  obtain judgment approving the Townshi p ’ s affordable housing pl an ; and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   Resolution   156-15,   the   Township   commenced   a   declaratory  judgment   action   captioned   In   Re   Township   of   West   Orange ,   Docket   No.   ESX-L-4697-15   (the  “ Declaratory Judgment Action ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   Redwood,   LLC.   ( “ Redwood ” ),   the   owner   of   a   6-acre   property   located   at   200  Pleasant   Valley   Way   at   Block   1 51 ,   Lot   3 3   (the   “ Redwood   Property ” )   intervened   into   the  Declaratory   Judgment   Action   to   challenge   the   Township ’ s   affordable   housing   plan   as   related   to  Redwood ’ s interes t in developing the   Redwo od P roperty; and  WHEREAS ,   West   Essex   Highlands,   Inc.   ( “ WEHI ” ),   the   owner   of   a   125-acre   property  located   in   the   northwest   area   of   the   Township   at   Block   179,   Lot   32   (the   “ WEHI   Property ” )  intervened   into   the   Declaratory   Judgment   Actio n   to   challenge   the   Townsh ip ’ s   affordable   housing  plan as related to WEHI ’ s interest in developing the WEHI Property ; and  WHEREAS ,   WEHI ’ s   challenge   relates   to   the   number   of   units   they   seek   to   build   at   the  WEHI Property ; and  WHEREAS,   the   potential   impact   on   the   traffic   in   the   area   o f   the   WEHI   Property   is   a  substantial concern in the analysis of these projects ;   and  WHEREAS,   on   May   8,   2018,   the   Township   Council   adopted   Resolution   114-18  authorizing   the   Township   to   retain   CME   Associates   ( “ CME ” ),   a   consulting   an d   municipal

2  engineering   f irm   located   at   1460   Route   9   South,   Howell,   New   Jersey   07731   to   prepare   a   letter  in response to the WEHI traffic study; and  WHEREAS,   CME   prepared   a   letter   on   behalf   of   the   Township   c oncerning   the   WEHI  traffic study; and  WHEREAS,   in   response   to   CME ’ s   let ter,   WEHI   issued   a   supplemental   traffic   study   and  additional data to rebut CME ’ s response; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   now   requires   the   preparation   of   its   own   traffic   study   to  research the potential impact on the traffic in the a rea of the WEHI Property;   and  WHEREAS,   CME   has   submitted   a   proposal   to   perform   and   prepare   a   traffic   study,  annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A ”   (the   “ Proposal ” ) and  WHEREAS,   the   Proposal   provides   that   CME   will   prepare   the   traffic   study   at   a   cost   not   to  exceed   $ 13 , 3 00; and  WHEREAS,   the   Proposal   is   less   than   the   bid   threshold   thereby   exempting   the   proposal  from the requirements of the Local Public Contracts Law.  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Townsh ip   is   hereby   authorized   t o   retain   CME   to  prepare   a   traffic   report   pursuant   to   the   terms   of   the   Proposal   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A; ”  and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   Mayor   is   authorized   to   execute   the   Proposal   and   any   other   necessary  documents   to   retain   CME   consistent   with   the   p roposals   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A ; ”   and   be  it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Municipal   Clerk   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   attest   to   the  Mayor ’ s signature; and be it further

3  RESOLVED   this   resolution   shall   be   published   and   mad e   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   in  accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. 263-18  November 2 7, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Housing   Rehabilitation   Program   has   a  mortgage   against   certain   real   property   owned   by   John   Stein   and   Sandra   Stein   (the   “ Owner s ” )  residing   at   4   Pillot   Street ,   Block   24 ,   Lot   49   (the   “ Property ” ),   in   the   amount   o f   Thirty-Five  Thousand   Dollars   ($ 3 5 , 000 . 0 0),   which   was   dated   January   11 ,   20 13   and   recorded   in   the   Ess ex  County   Register ’ s   Office   on   March   26 ,   20 13   in   Book   1 4242 ,   Page   90 59   (the   “ WOHRP  Mortgage ” ); and  WHEREAS,   there   is   a   first   mortgage   on   the   Property   held   by   BSI   Financial   ( “ BSI ” )   in   the  outstanding   amount   of   One   Hundred   Eleven   Thousand   Nine   Hundred   Twenty-Four   Dollars  ($ 111,924 . 00 )   (the   “ First   Mortgage ” )   for   which   the   Owners   are   required   to   pay   $1,304   per  month;   and  WHEREAS,   there   is   also   a   second   mortgage   on   the   Property   held   by   TD   Bank   ( “ TD  Bank ” )   in   the   outstanding   amount   of   Thirty-Two   Thousand   Four   Hundred   Ninety-Six   Dollars  ($32,496.00)   (the   “ Second   Mortgage ” )   for   which   the   Owners   are   required   to   pay   $306   per  month; and  WHEREAS,   a   foreclosure   action   wa s   brought   against   the   Owners   for   their   failure   to  make   time ly   payments   towards   the   First   Mortgage,   the   Owner s   desire   to   obtain   a   reverse  mortgage   from   Advisors   Mortgage   Group,   LLC   ( “ Advisors   Mortgage ” )   in   the   amount   of   One  Hundred   Thirty   Thousand   Five   Hund red   Thirty   ($ 130 , 530 .00) ,   and   with   an   interest   rate   of  5 . 044 %   (the   “ Reverse   Mortgage ” )   in   order   to   allow   them   to   continue   to   reside   in   the   Property ;  and  WHEREAS,   the   Reverse   Mortgage   will   provide   funds   towards   pay ing   off   the   First  Mortgage   and   the   Second   M ortgage   with   the   Owners   paying   the   remaining   balance   due   on   the  First Mortgage and Second Mortgage ; and

2  WHEREAS ,   the   Owner s   ha ve   requested   that   the   Township   of   West   Orange   subordinate  the WOHRP Mortgage to the   Reverse   Mortgage; and  WHEREAS , the   appraised v alue of the property is $ 295 , 000 ; and  WHEREAS,   based   on   the   amount   of   the   Reverse   Mortgage   as   compared   to   the   First  Mortgage   and   Second   Mortgage ,   the   proposed   subordination   will   not   materially   change   the  equity available to satisfy the WOHRP Mortgage;  NOW   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Mayor   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   execute   a  Subordination   of   Mortgage,   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A, ”   in   favor   of   Advisors   Mortgage   with  respect to the Property   and the Owner s ; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Municipal   Clerk   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   attest   to   the  Mayor ’ s signature on the Subordination of Mortgage ; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   th e   Clerk ’ s   office  in accordance with applicable law. NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   that   the   Mayor   shall   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   execute  the   Amendment   annexed   hereto   as   Attachment   “ B ” ,   and   the   Municipal   Clerk   shall   be   and  hereby is authorized to attest to the Mayor ’ s signature; and  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED ,   that   the   original   fully   executed   cop y   of   this   Resolution  and   the   executed   Amendment   to   the   Lease   with   the   initial   executed   copy   of   the   original   L icense  between the Township and   NJ Transit . 1  266-18  November 2 7, 2018  RESOLUTION  A   R esolution Authorizing the Planning Board to Undertake a Preliminary  Investigation to Determine Whether   Block   68 , Lot   1 Qualifies for Designation as a  Non-Condemnation   Area   in Need of Redevelopment Pursuant to   N.J.S.A.   40A:12A-1 et. seq.  WH EREAS,   the   Local   Redevelopment   and   Housing   Law,   N.J.S.A.   40A:12A-1   et   seq.,  provides   a   mechanism   to   empower   and   assist   local   governments   in   efforts   to   promote   programs  of redevelopment; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   desires   to   e xplore   whether  the   real   propert y   located   at   46   Mt.   Pleasant   Avenue,   West   Orange,   New   Jersey   and   more  commonly   known   as   Block   68 ,   Lot   1   on   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Tax   Map,   inclusive   of   any  and   all   streets,   “ paper ”   streets,   private   drives   and   right   of   wa ys   (the   “ Study   Area ” )   may   be   an  appropriate area for consideration for   r edevelopment;   and  WHEREAS,   the   Local   Redevelopment   and   Housing   Law   sets   forth   a   specific   procedure  for establishing an area in need of redevelopment; and  WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A .   40A:12A-6,   prior   to   the   Township   making   a  determination   as   to   whether   the   Study   Area   qualifies   as   an   area   in   need   of   redevelopment,   the  Township   Council   must   authorize   the   Planning   Board,   by   resolution,   to   undertake   a   preliminary  investigation   to   determine   whether   the   Study   Area   meets   the   criteria   of   an   area   in   need   of  redevelopment set forth in   N.J.S.A . 40A:12A-5; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   Council   wishes   to   direct   the   Township   of   West   Ora n ge  Planning   Board   to   undertake   such   preliminary   investigation   to   det ermine   whether   the   Study  Area   meets   the   criteria   for   designation   as   an   area   in   need   of   redevelopment   pursuant   to  N.J.S.A .   40A:12A-5   and   in   accordance   with   the   investigation   and   hearing   process   set   forth   at  N.J.S.A . 40A:12A-6 ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   Coun cil   hereby   states   that   any   redevelopment   area  determination   shall   authorize   the   municipality   to   use   all   those   powers   provided   by   the   Local

2  Redevelopment   and   Housing   Law   for   use   in   a   redevelopment   area,   except   the   use   of   eminent  domain (hereinafter referred   to as a "Non-Condemnation Redevelopment Area"); and  WHEREAS,   the   notice   of   any   hearing   to   be   conducted   by   the   Township   of   West   Orange  Planning   Board   with   regards   to   this   Resolution   shall   specifically   state   that   a   Non-Condemnation  Redevelopment   Area   deter mination   shall   not   authorize   the   municipality   to   exercise   the   power   of  eminent domain to acquire any p roperty   in the Study Area .  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   that   the   Township   Council   hereby   directs   the  West   Orange   Township   Planning   Board   to   conduct   the   necessary   investigation   and   to   hold   a  public   hearing   to   determine   whether   the   Study   Area   defined   hereinabove   qualifies   for  designation   as   an   area   in   need   of   redevelopment   under   the   criteria   and   pursuant   to   the   public  hearing process set forth in   N.J.S.A . 4 0A:12A-1,   et seq. ; and  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   the   redevelopment   area   determination   shall   further  authorize   the   municipality   to   use   all   those   powers   provided   by   the   New   Jersey   Legislature   for  use in a redevelopment area .  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   the   Township   Council   hereby   states   that   any   Non-  Condemnation   Redevelopment   Area   determination   shall   authorize   the   municipality   to   use   all  those   powers   provided   by   the   Local   Redevelopment   and   Housing   Law   for   use   in   a  redevelopment area, except the use of emine nt domain.  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   the   notice   of   any   hearing   to   be   conducted   by   the  Township   of   West   Orange   Planning   Board   with   regards   to   this   Resolution   shall   specifically   state  that   a   Non-Condemnation   Redevelopment   Area   determination   shall   not   autho rize   the  municipality to exercise the power of eminent domain to acquire any property in the Study Area.  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   the   Planning   Board   shall   submit   its   findings   and  recommendations   to   the   Township   Council   in   the   form   of   a   Resolution   with   s upportive  documentation.

3  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   if   the   Township   Council   designates   the   Study   Area  as   an   area   in   need   of   redevelopment   it   hereby   authorizes   the   Planning   Board   to   prepare   a  redevelopment plan which shall be subject to the review and appr oval of the Township Council  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   a   certified   copy   of   this   Resolution   be   forwarded   to   the  Township   Clerk,   Chief   Financial   Officer   and   Township   Planning   Board   and   Planning   Board  Secretary.  RESOLVED,   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   sha ll   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office  in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. Carneva le ,   R .M.C.   Susan McCartney  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: November 27, 2018  4833-6402-1068, v. 1
