AUTHORIZING PLANNING BOARD TO UNDERTAKE PRELIMINARY INVESTIGATION AS TO QUALIFICATION OF WEST ORANGE LIBRARY AS AN AREA IN NEED O~1
AUTHORIZING RETENTION OF HEYER GRUEL FOR POSSIBLE REDEVELOPMENT OF WEST ORANGE PUBLIC LIBRARY

pages.text: 267-18  November 27, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   seeks   to   explore   whether   certain   real  property   located   46   Mt.   Pleasant   Avenue,   West   Orange,   New   Jersey   and   more   commonly   known   as  Block   68 ,   Lot   1   on   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Tax   Map,   incl usive   of   any   and   all   streets,   “ paper ”  streets,   private   drives   and   right   of   ways   (the   “ Study   Area ” )   may   be   an   appropriate   area   for   consideration  for redevelopment;   and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   seeks   to   retain   Heyer,   Gruel   &   Associates   ( “ Heyer   Gruel ” )   to   perfo rm  the   evaluation   as   to   whether   the   Study   Area   meets   the   qualification   under   the   Municipal   Land   Use   Law  (the   “ MLUL ” ) as an area in need of redevelopment; and  WHEREAS,   Heyer   Gruel   has   provided   a   proposal   for   the   preparation   and   presentation   of   an  evaluation   and   report   as   to   whether   the   Study   Area   meets   qualification   under   the   MLUL,   annexed   hereto  as Exhibit   “ A; ”   and  WHEREAS ,   the   proposal   for   the   preparation   and   presentation   of   an   evaluation   and   report   as   to  whether the Study Area meets qualification unde r th e MLUL shall be capped at $8 ,0 0 0 .  NOW   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP  OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   retain   Heyer   Gruel   to   perform   the  evaluation   as   to   whether   the   Study   Area   constitutes   a n   Area   in   Need   of   Redevelopment   consistent   with  the Proposal annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A . ”   and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Mayor   is   authorized   to   execute   all   necessary   agreements   or   documents ,   if  any,   to retain   Heyer Gruel   consistent with the Propos al annexed hereto as   Exhibit   “ A ; ”   and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Municipal   Clerk   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   attest   to   the   Mayor ’ s  signature ; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   offi ce   in  accordance with applicable law.  Karen   J. Carnevale , R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Ado pted: November 27, 2018  4836-8080-6990, v. 1
Exhibit   “A”

236 BROAD STREET, RED BANK, NJ 07701   •   TEL 732.741.2900   •   FAX 732.741.2929   •   MAIL@HGAPA.COM   •   www.HGAPA.com  November 19, 2018  Richard Trenk  McManimon, Scotland & Baumann, LLC  75 Livingston Avenue  Roseland, NJ 07068  RE: Proposal for Professional Planning Services  West Orange Area in Need of Redevelopment Study   –   Block 68, Lot 1  Dear Mr. Trenk:  We are pleased to submit this proposal to provide professional planning services associated with the preparation of an Area in Need of Redevelopment Investigation for Block 68, Lot 1 in the Township. Our firm is well versed in the preparation of redevelopment investigations throughout the State. We have extensive experience working with the Local Redevelopment and Housing Law (LRHL) and frequently sit on panels dealing with redevelopment law topics.  The scope of work for the project will include a site visit and inspection, collection and review of relevant Township records, additional research as appropriate, and the preparation of a written document describing the property and evaluating it with regard to the criteria outlined in the LRHL.  It is our understanding that a facility study / capital improvement assessment has been prepared for the library building. Based upon the assumption that we will be able to rely on information in the capital improvement assessment, our fee for preparation of the Redevelopment Study would not exceed a total of $8,000 including attendance at one Planning Board meeting. All other meetings and preparation (e.g. internal, additional public) will be billed separately per meeting at our hourly rates. Firm principal Susan Gruel will attend all public meetings and present the Plan at the Planning Board in accordance with the statutory requirements.  We welcome the opportunity to work with the Township on this important redevelopment project. Should you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact us.  Sincerely yours,  HEYER, GRUEL & ASSOCIATES  Fred Heyer, PP, AICP LEED- AP ND, CNUa  Principal  Susan S. Gruel, PP  Principal

2556 - 18  BOND   ORDINANCE   PROVIDING   FOR   THE   LOCAL  UNIT’S   ALLOCABLE   SHARE   OF   CAPITAL  IMPROVEMENT PROJECTS FOR THE JOINT MEETING  OF   ESSEX   AND   UNION   COUNTIES,   BY   AND   IN   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST OR ANGE , IN THE COUNTY OF  ESSEX ,   STATE   OF   NEW   JERSEY,   APPROPRIATING  $ 1,5 70,000   TH EREFOR   AND   AUTHORIZING   THE  ISSUANCE   OF   $ 1,570,000   BONDS   OR   NOTES   TO  FINANCE THE COST THEREOF  BE   IT ORDAINED AND ENACTED BY THE   GOVERNING   BODY OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST ORANGE , I N THE COUNTY OF   ESSEX , STATE OF NEW  JERSEY (not less than two - thirds   of all th e members thereof affirmatively concurring),  AS FOLLOWS:  SECTION   1 .   The   improvements   described   in   Section   3   of   this   bond  ordinance are hereby authorized to be undertaken by the   Joint Meeting of Essex and  Union Counties (the “Joint Meeting”) on be half of t he   Township   of   West Orange , in the  County of   Essex , State of New Jersey (the "Local Unit") as general improvements.   For  the said improvements stated in Section 3, there is hereb y appropriated the aggregate  sum of $ 1,570,000 .   Pursuant to the prov isions of   N.J.S.A. 40A:2 - 11(c) of the Local  Bond Law, N.J.S.A. 40A:2 - 1   et   seq ., as amended and supplemented (the “Local Bond  Law”), no down payment is required as this bond ordinance auth orizes obligations for  environmental infrastructure projects which ar e   funded by a loan from the New Jersey  Infrastructure Bank (the “I - Bank”).  SECTION   2 .   For   the   financing   of   said   improvements   or   purposes  described in Section 3 hereof and to meet the $ 1, 570,000   appropriation, negotiable  bonds of the Local Unit are hereby   au thorized to be issued in the principal amount of

$ 1,570,000   pursuant to and within all limitations prescribed by the Local Bond Law.   In  anticipation of the issuance of said bonds and t o temporarily finance said improvements  or purposes, negotiable notes   o f the Local Unit in a principal amount not exceeding  $ 1,570,000   are hereby authorized to be issued pursuant to and within the limitations  prescribed by said Local Bond Law.  SECTION   3 .   The improvements hereby authorized and purposes for the  financing of   wh ich said debt obligations are to be issued are for the Local Unit’s  allocable share of capital improvement projects being undertaken by the Joint Meeting,  including,   but   not   limited   to,   (i)   waste   gas   burner   upgrades   consisting   of   (a)   the  replacement of w as te gas burners at the Digester Building and (b) upgrades to sludge  storage   tanks   with   state - of - the - art   flares   to   meet   current   air   emission   permitting  requirements;   (ii)   rehabilitation   o f   various   structures,   including,   but   not   limited   to,  concrete repairs   a nd/or reconstruction of the Screen House, the Old Chlorine Building,  the   Chlorination   Building,   the   Aeration   Gallery,   and   the   Waste   Gas   Building,  replacement of brick veneer panels and   supporting walls in the Dewatering Building,  and replacement of the c op ing and brick façade on the sludge storage tanks; and (iii)  replacement and construction of the Tunnel Building along with the replacement of  severely corroded piping within the tunnel,   including installation of new sludge pumps.  a.   All   improvements   sha ll   include,   as   applicable,   all   work,  materials, equipment,   engineering, design, architectural, environmental consulting work,  preparation   of   plans   and   specifications,   permits,   bid   documen ts,   conducting   and  preparation of reports and studies, equipment rent al , labor and appurtenances necessary  therefore or incidental thereto.

b.   The aggregate estimated maximum amount of bonds or notes to be  issued for said improvements or purposes is $ 1 ,570,000 .  c.   The aggregate estimated cost of said   improvements or pu rposes is  $ 1,570,000 .  SECTION 4.   In the event the United States of America, the State of New  Jersey, and/or the County of   Essex   make a loan, contribution or grant - in - aid to the Local  Uni t for the improvements authorized hereby and the sa me shall be receiv ed by the  Local Unit prior to the issuance of the bonds or notes authorized in Section 2 hereof,  then the amount of such bonds or notes to be issued shall be reduced by the amount so  rece ived from the United States of America, the State o f New Jersey, and/ or the County  of   Essex .   In the event, however, that any amount so loaned, contributed or granted by  the United States of America, the State of New Jersey, and/or the County of   Essex   shal l  be received by the Local Unit after the issuance   of the bonds or no tes authorized in  Section 2 hereof, then such funds shall be applied to the payment of the bonds or notes  so issued and shall be used for no other purpose.   This Section 4 shall not apply  however, with respect to any contribution or grant   in aid received b y the Local Unit as a  result of using such funds from this bond ordinance as “matching local funds” to receive  such contribution or grant in aid.  SECTION 5.   All bond anticipation notes i ssued hereunder shall mature at  such times as may b e determined by th e Chief Financial Officer of the Local Unit,  provided that no bond anticipation note shall mature later than one (1) year from its  date, unless such bond anticipation notes are permitted   to mature at such later date in

accordance with the   provisions of the   New Jersey Infrastructure Trust Act, N.J.S.A.  58:11B - 1 (“NJIT Act”).   The bond anticipation notes shall bear interest at such rate or  rates and be in such form as may be determined by th e Chief Financial Officer.   The  Chief Financial Off icer of the Local   Unit shall determine all matters in connection with  the bond anticipation notes issued pursuant to this bond ordinance, and the signature of  the   Chief   Financial   Officer   upon   the   bond   anti cipation   notes   shall   be   conclusive  evidence as to a ll such determinat ions.   All bond anticipation notes issued hereunder  may be renewed from time to time in accordance with the provisions of Section 8.1 of  the Local Bond Law and, as applicable, the provisi ons of the NJIT Act.   The Chief  Financial Officer i s hereby authorize d to sell part or all of the bond anticipation notes  from time to time at public or private sale and to deliver them to the purchaser thereof  upon receipt of payment of the purchase price   and accrued interest thereon from their  dates to t he date of deliver y thereof.   The Chief Financial officer is directed to report in  writing to the governing body at the meeting next succeeding the date when any sale or  delivery of the bond anticipation n otes pursuant to this bond ordinance is made.   Such  report must inclu de the principal amount, the description, the interest rate, and the  maturity schedule of the bond anticipation notes so sold, the price obtained and the  name of the purchaser.  SECTION 6 .   The Local Unit hereby certifies that it has adopt ed a capital  budge t or a temporary capital budget, as applicable. The capital or temporary capital  budget of the Local Unit is hereby amended to conform with the provisions of this bond  ordinance to the ex tent of any inconsistency herewith.   To the extent   that the purposes  authorized herein are inconsistent with the adopted capital or temporary capital budget,

a revised capital or temporary capital budget has been filed with the Division of Local  Government   Services .  SECTION 7.   The   following   additional   mat ters   are   hereby   de termined,  declared, recited and stated:  a.   The purposes described in Section 3 of this bond ordinance are not  current expenses and are improvements which the Local Unit may lawfully unde rtake as  general improvements, and no part of the c ost thereof has be en or shall be specially  assessed on property specially benefited thereby.  b.   The average period of usefulness of said improvements is within  the   limitations   of   said   Local   Bond   Law,   acco rding   to   the   reasonable   life   thereof  computed from   the date of the sa id bonds authorized by this bond ordinance, is 30  years.  c.   The supplemental debt statement required by the Local Bond Law  has been duly made and filed in the Office of the Clerk of the   Local Unit and a complete  executed duplicate thereo f has been filed i n the Office of the Director of the Division of  Local Government Services within the Department of Community Affairs of the State of  New Jersey, and such statement shows that the gross de bt of the Local Unit, as defined  in the Local Bond   Law, is increased   by the authorization of the bonds and notes  provided for in this bond ordinance by $ 1,570,000   and the said obligations authorized  by this bond ordinance will be within all debt limitation s prescribed by said Local Bond  Law.  d.   An aggrega te amount not exce eding $ 180,000   for items of expense  listed in and permitted under section 20 of the Local Bond Law is included in the

estimated cost indicated herein for the improvements hereinbefore des cribed.  e.   In   accordance   with   N.J.S.A.   40:63 - 134,   bonds   authorized   a nd  issued by the Local Unit to finance improvements or works for the Joint Meeting shall  not be taken to be included within or governed by any limitation fixed by law to the  amount of bon ds authorized to be issued by the Local Unit.  SECT ION 8.   The full fa ith and credit of the Local Unit are hereby pledged  to the punctual payment of the principal of and the interest on the debt obligations  authorized by this bond ordinance.   The debt oblig ations shall be direct, unlimited  obligations of th e Local Unit, and   the Local Unit shall be obligated to levy ad valorem  taxes upon all the taxable property within the Local Unit for the payment of the debt  obligations and the interest thereon without lim itation as to rate or amount.  SECTION 9.   The   Local   Unit   reasonably   e xpects   to   reimburse   any  expenditures toward the costs of the improvements or purposes described in Section 3  hereof and paid prior to the issuance of any bonds or notes authorized by this   bond  ordinance with the proceeds of such bonds or   notes.   This Secti on 9 is intended to be  and   hereby   is   a   declaration   of   the   Local   Unit's   official   intent   to   reimburse   any  expenditures toward the costs of the improvements or purposes described in Section   3  hereof to be incurred and paid prior to the issua nce of bonds or no tes authorized herein  in accordance with Treasury Regulations Section 150 - 2.  SECTION 10.   The Local Unit covenants to maintain the exclusion from gross  income under section 103(a) of the   Code, of the interest on all bonds and notes issue d  under this ordin ance.  SECTION 11.   Upon adoption hereof, the Clerk of the Local Unit shall forward

certified copies of this bond ordinance to Everett M. Johnson, Esq., Wilentz, Goldman &  Spitzer, P.A.,   bond counsel to the Joint Meeting of Essex and Union Counties, and  Ri chard T . Nolan, Esq., McCarter & English, LLP, bond counsel to the I - Bank.  SECTION 12.   This bond ordinance shall take effect twenty (20) days after the first  publication hereof after fi nal adoption, as provided by the Local Bond Law.  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, R.M.C.   ROBERT D. PARISI , MAYOR  MUNICIPAL CLERK  SUSAN McCARTNEY , COUNCIL PRESIDENT  I NTRODUCED:   November 13, 2018  ADOPTED:   November 27, 2018

It is the purpose of this Ordinance to aban don, release and extinguish the public  rights, including any right to build a public roadway, in Elmwood Avenue between Roosevelt Avenue and  Garfield Avenue. 2557-18  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance vacates Elmwood Avenue between Roosevelt Avenue and Garfield Avenue which is a  paper street . The vacation of the right of way releases and extinguishes any and all public rights to this  portion of Elmwood Aven ue including barring the right to build a public road within the vacated right of  way. Although this portion of Elmwood Avenue will be vacated, access to property owned by the  Township of West Orange that could previously be accessed through Elmwood Avenu e may still be  accessed through other public rights of way including Garfield Avenue and Mt. Vernon Avenue. The  vacated section of the Elmwood Avenue right of way between Roosevelt Avenue and Garfield Avenue  will revert to the adjoining property owners wh ich includes the Township of West Orange and the  owners of Block 172.10, Lot 2 and Block 172.05, Lot 8 commonly known as 100 Roosevelt Avenue and  102 Roosevelt Avenue respectively. Since a stream currently exists in a portion of vacated Elmwood  Avenue a 2 0 foot wide easement will be created around the centerline of the vacated right of way for  drainage and future utility purposes.

255-18  No vember 13, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS , the Township of West Orange desires to replace the underground storage tanks for  diesel fuel and gasoline at Fire House No. 4 at 280 Pleasant Valley Way with a n e w above ground storage  tank with compartments and dispensers for each fuel; and  WHEREAS , simultaneous to the removal of the two existing tanks, the Township desires to  remove and replace any contaminated soils underneath and alongside the existing tanks   as well as in  areas between the tanks and the location of former fuel dispensers; and  WHEREAS , the firm of T. Slack Environmental Services, 180 Market Street, Kenilworth, New  Jersey 07033 submitted a proposal   dated October 10, 2018 to provide existing ta nk removal,  contaminated soil removal and new above ground tank installation based on their prices in the Bergen  County C o operative Purchasing Contract No. 18 -08,   and  WHEREAS , the fees for these services based on that Cooperative Contract are as follows  1.   Underground Storage Tank Removal   $   5,943.00  2.   Above Ground Storage Tank Installation   $110,554.00  3.   Contaminated Soil Removal and   $ 22,500.00  Replacement allowance   _____________  TOTAL   $138,997.00 ;   and  WHEREAS , the To wnship is a member of the Bergen County Cooperative; and  WHEREAS , the proposal of T. Slack   Environmental Services meets with the approval of the  Township Engineer ;   and  WHEREAS , sufficient funds exist   for this work; and  WHEREAS , the Township Council concurs in the foregoing

NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the   Township Council of the Township of West Orange  that a contract for underground storage tank removal, contaminated s oil removal and replacement and  above ground storage tank installation be awarded to T. Slack Environmental Services, Inc., 180 Market  Street, Kenilworth, New Jersey 07033 in the amount of $138,997.00 in accordance with their proposal  dated October 10, 201 8 based on pricing under the Bergen County Cooperative Purchasing Contract No.  18-08.  Karen J. Carnevale, RMC, Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney ,   Council President  Adopted:   Nove mber 13, 2018  I certify funds are available :   Account Number   -   f rom the following account  03 - 2507 - 17 - 0600 -0 10  $138,997.00  John O. 256-18  No vember 13, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS , The Township of West Orange obtained the service s   of Manal Baba, Licensed Site  Remediation Professional (LSRP) ,   of CME Associates of Monmouth Junction, New Jersey   and   formerly of  T&M Associates of Middletown, New Jersey to   provide professional services to remediate contaminated  soil and groundwater at the Public Works Building, 25 Lakeside Avenue, associated with a former lea k ing  underground storage tank; and  WHEREAS , to maintain compliance with New Jersey ’ s Site Remediatio n Reform Act, additional  Soil Sampling and Monitoring Well Gauging and Sampling of groundwater are required; and  WHEREAS , the purpose of this additional G roundwater   Sampling   and Soil Sampling   is to  demonstrate the natural degrading of the contaminants an d to use the   Soil Samples for Compliance  Averaging to further demonstrate compliance with the NJDEP Technical Requirements for Site  Remediation; and  WHEREAS , the goal of this work is for the LSRP to issue a Response Action Outcome (RAO) which  will state   that   contaminate d   areas of concern were remediated in accordance with the applicable  regulations, and that no further remedial action is required; and  WHEREAS , the firm of CME ASSOCIATES prepared a proposal dated November 6, 2018 to  provide services for   Soil Sampling, Monitoring Well Gauging and Sampling, Classification Exception Area  (CEA) Application, Contaminant Transport Modeling and Groundwater ,   Remedial Action Permit  R eporting ,   Project Management and Issuance   of   a Response Action Outcome (RAO) for a   fee not to  exceed $59,500.00; and  WHEREAS , CME Associates responded to the Township ’ s Request for Professional Qualifications;  and  WHEREAS , the Municipal Engineer finds the proposal acceptable; and

WHEREAS , sufficient funds exist for this work; and  WHEREAS , the Township Council concurs in the foregoing  NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange  that a Professional Services contract be awarded to CME Associates, 3759 Highway 1 South, Suite 100,  Monmouth J unction, New Jersey 08852, Manal Baba, LSRP, to provide professional services associated  with the remediation of   contaminated   soil and groundwater at the Public Works Building, 25 Lakeside  Avenue from a former   leaking underground storage tank in accordance   with their proposal dated  November 6, 2018 for a fee not to exceed $59,500.00.  Karen J. Carnevale, RMC, Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney ,   Council President  Adopted:   Nove mber 13, 2018  I certify funds are available :   Account Number   -   f rom the following account  03 - 2507 - 17 - 0600 -0 10  $59,500.00  John O. Gross, Chief Financial Officer

2 5 8 -18  November 13 , 2018  RESOLUTION TO HOLD A CLOSED SESSION  WHEREAS , the Open Public Meetings Act (N.J.S.A. 10:4. et seq.) provides for the exclusion of  the public meetings of the governing body during the discussion of certain matters; and  WHEREAS , prior to the exclusion of the public from a meeting of the Township Council it is  required that the Council adopt a Resolution stating the general nature of the subject to be discussed and  stating as precisely as possible the time whe n the minutes of the discussion conducted in closed session   can  be disclosed to the public; and  WHERE AS , this body is about to consider a matter which falls within the purview of N.J.S.A.  10-4-12, and can properly exclude the public from such discussion s; now, therefore, be it  BE IT   RESOLVED , that the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, now assembled  in public session   at   its regular meeting of   November 13 , 2018   enter into a closed session to consider the  following matter(s).  ( )   1.   Discussion of any material, the disclosure of which constitutes an   i nvasion of individual  privacy .  (   )   2 .   A collective bargaining agreement or the terms of provisions of same.  ( )   3.   Techniques or tactic utilized to pro tect the safety and property of the public provided that  their disclosure could impair such protection.  ( )   4.   An investigation   of violations o r   possible violations of the laws of the State of New  Jersey   and/ or   the Township of West   Orange Administrative Code.  ( X )   5.   Pending or anticipated litigation or contract negotiation in which this body is   or maybe a  party.  ( )   6.   A matter falling within the attorney-client privilege to the extent that confidentiality   is  r equired in order for the attorney to   exercise his ethical duties to this body.  ()   7.   Personnel matters.  (   )   8.   Discussion of any matter which by express provision of Federal law or State statute or  rule of Court shall be render ed confidential.  ( )   9.   Discussion   of any matter in which the release of information would impair   a right to  receive funds from the Government of the United Sates.  ( )   10 .   Discussion of any matter involving the purchase, lease or acquisition   of   real property  with public funds, the setting   of banking   rates or investment of public funds, where it  could adversely affect the public interest if discussion of such matters were disclosed.  ( )   11.   Deliberations occurring after a public he aring that may result in imposition of a civil  penalty or suspension or loss of a license or permit.

More specifically, the Council   may   discuss in closed session the following topic ( s ) :  Resolution   authorizing settlement agreement in a matter sealed by the Court.  (the general subject matter ( s ) ) of d iscussion shall be indicated by a check mark in the appropriate box and  shall be more specifically referred to thereafter to the extent suc h   additional disclosure can be made without  compromising the need for confidentiality giving rise to the closed sessi on.  IT IS FURTH E R   RESOLVED   that   the minutes of said discussion shall be made public as  soon as the matter under discussion is no longer of a confidential or sensitive nature such that the public  interest will no longer be served by such confidentiality.  The foregoing   r esolution was duly adopted by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange at a  public meeting held   on   November 13, 2018  Motion :  Second :  Vote  Aye :  Opposed :  Karen J. Carnevale , R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  M unic ipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   November 13, 2018

2555-18  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING   CHAPTER 7, TRAFFIC, SUBSECT I ON   7-37.1b MUNICIPAL PARKING YARDS;  METERED PARKING, PERMIT PARKING, TIME LIMITED PARKING ,  AND   7-23.1 TURNS RESTRICTED   OF THE REVISED GENERAL ORDINANCES  OF   THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  BE IT ORDAINED,   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, in the County of Essex, State of New Jersey as  follows:  Section 1.   Subsection   7-37.1b ,   Municipal Parking Yards ,   of the   Revised General Ordinances of the   Township of West  Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows: ( italic type denotes deletion,   bold type denotes addition):  Parking Yard   Block   Lot   Location  13   176.05   12   (portion) and   463-481 Pleasant Valley Way  14 ( portion)  13   176.05   12.13   4 Haller Road (behind 471 Pleasant Valley Way)  16   176.03   10   665 Eagle Rock Avenue  Section 2.   Subsection 7-23.1, Turn Prohibitions, of the Revised General Ordinances of the  Township of West Orange   is hereby supplemented and amended as follows: (italic type denotes deletion,   bold  type denotes addition):  Intersection   Turn Prohibited   Hours/Days   Movement Prohibited  4 Haller Road   Left   All   Westbound out of driveway of  (Driveway of   4 Haller Road   for Parking Yard 13  Parking Yard 13)   to southwesterly   on Haller Road.  Section 3 .   If any provisions of this Ordinance or application thereof , under any circumstances, is  held invalid, the invalidity shall not affect any of the provisions or applications of this Ordinance that can be given  effect without the valid provision(s) or application and to this end, the provisions of this Ordinance are severable.  Section 4 .   All other Ordinances or parts of Ordinances inconsistent with this Ordinance are hereby repealed.  Section   5 .   This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication   in accordance with law .  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor   Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C., Municipal Clerk  Susan McCartney, Council President  Introduced:   October 30, 2018  Adopted:   November 13, 2018

2555-18  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  Parking Yard   # 13 ,   previously located at 463-481 Pleasant Valley Way, has been relocated to  4 Haller Road due to construction of the ne w   Chase Bank on that site. The Land Use Board approval for  the Chase Bank at 471   Pleasant Valley Way required that left turns out of the rear Municipal Parking  Yard be prohibited.  New Parking Yard   # 16, located at 665 Eagle Rock Avenue,   is   located on the westbound side  behind the Pleasant Valley Way businesses , and is utilized   by local residents patronizing those  businesses. This lot has been newly constructed, replacing parking spaces lost due to the construction of  the Chase Bank.

2556 - 18  BOND   ORDINANCE   PROVIDING   FOR   THE   LOCAL  UNIT’S   ALLOCABLE   SHARE   OF   CAPITAL  IMPROVEMENT PROJECTS FOR THE JOINT MEETING  OF   ESSEX   AND   UNION   COUNTIES,   BY   AND   IN   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST OR ANGE , IN THE COUNTY OF  ESSEX ,   STATE   OF   NEW   JERSEY,   APPROPRIATING  $ 1,5 70,000   TH EREFOR   AND   AUTHORIZING   THE  ISSUANCE   OF   $ 1,570,000   BONDS   OR   NOTES   TO  FINANCE THE COST THEREOF  BE   IT ORDAINED AND ENACTED BY THE   GOVERNING   BODY OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST ORANGE , I N THE COUNTY OF   ESSEX , STATE OF NEW  JERSEY (not less than two - thirds   of all th e members thereof affirmatively concurring),  AS FOLLOWS:  SECTION   1 .   The   improvements   described   in   Section   3   of   this   bond  ordinance are hereby authorized to be undertaken by the   Joint Meeting of Essex and  Union Counties (the “Joint Meeting”) on be half of t he   Township   of   West Orange , in the  County of   Essex , State of New Jersey (the "Local Unit") as general improvements.   For  the said improvements stated in Section 3, there is hereb y appropriated the aggregate  sum of $ 1,570,000 .   Pursuant to the prov isions of   N.J.S.A. 40A:2 - 11(c) of the Local  Bond Law, N.J.S.A. 40A:2 - 1   et   seq ., as amended and supplemented (the “Local Bond  Law”), no down payment is required as this bond ordinance auth orizes obligations for  environmental infrastructure projects which ar e   funded by a loan from the New Jersey  Infrastructure Bank (the “I - Bank”).  SECTION   2 .   For   the   financing   of   said   improvements   or   purposes  described in Section 3 hereof and to meet the $ 1, 570,000   appropriation, negotiable  bonds of the Local Unit are hereby   au thorized to be issued in the principal amount of

$ 1,570,000   pursuant to and within all limitations prescribed by the Local Bond Law.   In  anticipation of the issuance of said bonds and t o temporarily finance said improvements  or purposes, negotiable notes   o f the Local Unit in a principal amount not exceeding  $ 1,570,000   are hereby authorized to be issued pursuant to and within the limitations  prescribed by said Local Bond Law.  SECTION   3 .   The improvements hereby authorized and purposes for the  financing of   wh ich said debt obligations are to be issued are for the Local Unit’s  allocable share of capital improvement projects being undertaken by the Joint Meeting,  including,   but   not   limited   to,   (i)   waste   gas   burner   upgrades   consisting   of   (a)   the  replacement of w as te gas burners at the Digester Building and (b) upgrades to sludge  storage   tanks   with   state - of - the - art   flares   to   meet   current   air   emission   permitting  requirements;   (ii)   rehabilitation   o f   various   structures,   including,   but   not   limited   to,  concrete repairs   a nd/or reconstruction of the Screen House, the Old Chlorine Building,  the   Chlorination   Building,   the   Aeration   Gallery,   and   the   Waste   Gas   Building,  replacement of brick veneer panels and   supporting walls in the Dewatering Building,  and replacement of the c op ing and brick façade on the sludge storage tanks; and (iii)  replacement and construction of the Tunnel Building along with the replacement of  severely corroded piping within the tunnel,   including installation of new sludge pumps.  a.   All   improvements   sha ll   include,   as   applicable,   all   work,  materials, equipment,   engineering, design, architectural, environmental consulting work,  preparation   of   plans   and   specifications,   permits,   bid   documen ts,   conducting   and  preparation of reports and studies, equipment rent al , labor and appurtenances necessary  therefore or incidental thereto.

b.   The aggregate estimated maximum amount of bonds or notes to be  issued for said improvements or purposes is $ 1 ,570,000 .  c.   The aggregate estimated cost of said   improvements or pu rposes is  $ 1,570,000 .  SECTION 4.   In the event the United States of America, the State of New  Jersey, and/or the County of   Essex   make a loan, contribution or grant - in - aid to the Local  Uni t for the improvements authorized hereby and the sa me shall be receiv ed by the  Local Unit prior to the issuance of the bonds or notes authorized in Section 2 hereof,  then the amount of such bonds or notes to be issued shall be reduced by the amount so  rece ived from the United States of America, the State o f New Jersey, and/ or the County  of   Essex .   In the event, however, that any amount so loaned, contributed or granted by  the United States of America, the State of New Jersey, and/or the County of   Essex   shal l  be received by the Local Unit after the issuance   of the bonds or no tes authorized in  Section 2 hereof, then such funds shall be applied to the payment of the bonds or notes  so issued and shall be used for no other purpose.   This Section 4 shall not apply  however, with respect to any contribution or grant   in aid received b y the Local Unit as a  result of using such funds from this bond ordinance as “matching local funds” to receive  such contribution or grant in aid.  SECTION 5.   All bond anticipation notes i ssued hereunder shall mature at  such times as may b e determined by th e Chief Financial Officer of the Local Unit,  provided that no bond anticipation note shall mature later than one (1) year from its  date, unless such bond anticipation notes are permitted   to mature at such later date in

accordance with the   provisions of the   New Jersey Infrastructure Trust Act, N.J.S.A.  58:11B - 1 (“NJIT Act”).   The bond anticipation notes shall bear interest at such rate or  rates and be in such form as may be determined by th e Chief Financial Officer.   The  Chief Financial Off icer of the Local   Unit shall determine all matters in connection with  the bond anticipation notes issued pursuant to this bond ordinance, and the signature of  the   Chief   Financial   Officer   upon   the   bond   anti cipation   notes   shall   be   conclusive  evidence as to a ll such determinat ions.   All bond anticipation notes issued hereunder  may be renewed from time to time in accordance with the provisions of Section 8.1 of  the Local Bond Law and, as applicable, the provisi ons of the NJIT Act.   The Chief  Financial Officer i s hereby authorize d to sell part or all of the bond anticipation notes  from time to time at public or private sale and to deliver them to the purchaser thereof  upon receipt of payment of the purchase price   and accrued interest thereon from their  dates to t he date of deliver y thereof.   The Chief Financial officer is directed to report in  writing to the governing body at the meeting next succeeding the date when any sale or  delivery of the bond anticipation n otes pursuant to this bond ordinance is made.   Such  report must inclu de the principal amount, the description, the interest rate, and the  maturity schedule of the bond anticipation notes so sold, the price obtained and the  name of the purchaser.  SECTION 6 .   The Local Unit hereby certifies that it has adopt ed a capital  budge t or a temporary capital budget, as applicable. The capital or temporary capital  budget of the Local Unit is hereby amended to conform with the provisions of this bond  ordinance to the ex tent of any inconsistency herewith.   To the extent   that the purposes  authorized herein are inconsistent with the adopted capital or temporary capital budget,

a revised capital or temporary capital budget has been filed with the Division of Local  Government   Services .  SECTION 7.   The   following   additional   mat ters   are   hereby   de termined,  declared, recited and stated:  a.   The purposes described in Section 3 of this bond ordinance are not  current expenses and are improvements which the Local Unit may lawfully unde rtake as  general improvements, and no part of the c ost thereof has be en or shall be specially  assessed on property specially benefited thereby.  b.   The average period of usefulness of said improvements is within  the   limitations   of   said   Local   Bond   Law,   acco rding   to   the   reasonable   life   thereof  computed from   the date of the sa id bonds authorized by this bond ordinance, is 30  years.  c.   The supplemental debt statement required by the Local Bond Law  has been duly made and filed in the Office of the Clerk of the   Local Unit and a complete  executed duplicate thereo f has been filed i n the Office of the Director of the Division of  Local Government Services within the Department of Community Affairs of the State of  New Jersey, and such statement shows that the gross de bt of the Local Unit, as defined  in the Local Bond   Law, is increased   by the authorization of the bonds and notes  provided for in this bond ordinance by $ 1,570,000   and the said obligations authorized  by this bond ordinance will be within all debt limitation s prescribed by said Local Bond  Law.  d.   An aggrega te amount not exce eding $ 180,000   for items of expense  listed in and permitted under section 20 of the Local Bond Law is included in the

estimated cost indicated herein for the improvements hereinbefore des cribed.  e.   In   accordance   with   N.J.S.A.   40:63 - 134,   bonds   authorized   a nd  issued by the Local Unit to finance improvements or works for the Joint Meeting shall  not be taken to be included within or governed by any limitation fixed by law to the  amount of bon ds authorized to be issued by the Local Unit.  SECT ION 8.   The full fa ith and credit of the Local Unit are hereby pledged  to the punctual payment of the principal of and the interest on the debt obligations  authorized by this bond ordinance.   The debt oblig ations shall be direct, unlimited  obligations of th e Local Unit, and   the Local Unit shall be obligated to levy ad valorem  taxes upon all the taxable property within the Local Unit for the payment of the debt  obligations and the interest thereon without lim itation as to rate or amount.  SECTION 9.   The   Local   Unit   reasonably   e xpects   to   reimburse   any  expenditures toward the costs of the improvements or purposes described in Section 3  hereof and paid prior to the issuance of any bonds or notes authorized by this   bond  ordinance with the proceeds of such bonds or   notes.   This Secti on 9 is intended to be  and   hereby   is   a   declaration   of   the   Local   Unit's   official   intent   to   reimburse   any  expenditures toward the costs of the improvements or purposes described in Section   3  hereof to be incurred and paid prior to the issua nce of bonds or no tes authorized herein  in accordance with Treasury Regulations Section 150 - 2.  SECTION 10.   The Local Unit covenants to maintain the exclusion from gross  income under section 103(a) of the   Code, of the interest on all bonds and notes issue d  under this ordin ance.  SECTION 11.   Upon adoption hereof, the Clerk of the Local Unit shall forward

certified copies of this bond ordinance to Everett M. Johnson, Esq., Wilentz, Goldman &  Spitzer, P.A.,   bond counsel to the Joint Meeting of Essex and Union Counties, and  Ri chard T . Nolan, Esq., McCarter & English, LLP, bond counsel to the I - Bank.  SECTION 12.   This bond ordinance shall take effect twenty (20) days after the first  publication hereof after fi nal adoption, as provided by the Local Bond Law.  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, R.M.C.   ROBERT D. PARISI , MAYOR  MUNICIPAL CLERK  SUSAN McCARTNEY , COUNCIL PRESIDENT  I NTRODUCED:   November 13, 2018  ADOPTED:   November 27, 2018

238 -18  October 30 , 2018  RESOLUTION TO HOLD A CLOSED SESSION  WHEREAS , the Open Public Meetings Act (N.J.S.A. 10:4. et seq.) provides for the exclusion of  the public meetings of the governing body during the discussion of certain matters; and  WHEREAS , prior to the exclusion of the public from a meeting of the Township Council it is  required that the Council adopt a Resolution stating the general nature of the subject to be discussed and  stating as precisely as possible the time when the minutes of the discussion conducted in closed session   can  be disclosed to the public; and  WHERE AS , this body is about to consider a matter which falls within the purview of N.J.S.A.  10-4-12, and can properly exclude the public from such discussions; now, therefore, be it  BE IT   RESOLVED , that the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, now assembled  in public session   at   its regular meeting of   October 30 , 2018   enter into a closed session to consider the  following matter(s).  ( )   1.   Discussion of any material, the disclosure of which constitutes an   i nvasion of individual  privacy .  (   )   2 .   A collective bargaining agreement or the terms of provisions of same.  ( )   3.   Techniques or tactic utilized to protect the safety and property of the public provided that  their disclosure could impair such protection.  ( )   4.   An investigation   of violations o r   possible violations of the laws of the State of New  Jersey   and/ or   the Township of West Orange Administrative Code.  ( X )   5.   Pending or anticipated litigation or contract negotiation in which this body is   or maybe a  party.  ( )   6.   A matter falling within the attorney-client privilege to the extent that confidentiality is  r equired in order for the attorney to   exercise his ethical duties to this body.  ()   7.   Personnel matters.  (   )   8.   Discussion of any matter which by express provision of Federal law or State statute or  rule of Court shall be rendered confidential.  ( )   9.   Discussion   of any matter in which the release of information would impair   a right to  receive funds from the Government of the United Sates.  ( )   10 .   Discussion of any matter involving the purchase, lease or acquisition   of   real property  with public funds, the setting   of banking   rates or investment of public funds, where it  could adversely affect the public interest if discussion of such matters were disclosed.  ( )   11.   Deliberations occurring after a public hearing that may result in imposition of a civil  penalty or suspension or loss of a license or permit.

More specifically, the Council   may   discuss in closed session the following topic ( s ) :  C ontract negotiation s  (the general subject matter ( s ) ) of discussion shall be indicated by a check mark in the appropriate box and  shall be more specifically referred to thereafter to the extent suc h   additional disclosure can be made without  compromising the need for confidentiality giving rise to the closed session.  IT IS FURTH E R   RESOLVED   that   the minutes of said discussion shall be made public as  soon as the matter under discussion is no longer of a confidential or sensitive nature such that the public  interest will no longer be served by such confidentiality.  The foregoing   r esolution was duly adopted by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange at a  public meeting held   on   October 30, 2018  Motion :  Second :  Vote  Aye :  Opposed :  Karen J. Carnevale , R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  M unicipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   October 30 , 2018

pages.text: 241-18  October 30, 2018  RESOLUTION TO AWARD A CONTRACT EXTENSION FOR SNOW  PLOWING & SNOW HAULING SERVICES FOR 2018-2019  WHEREAS,   Resolution No. 254-16 was authorized by the Township of West Orange Council for  Snow Plowing and Removal Services for year(s) 2016-2017 on November 22, 2016; and  WHEREAS,   the Township wishes to continue contracted plowing in Routes 1D-1 Korwel/Oaks  and 1G-1 and 1G-2 St. Cloud (2 trucks); and  WHEREAS,   the Township desires to extend the contract as allowed in the original bid document  giving the Township the option to extend the contract for two (2) one (1) year periods; and  WHEREAS,   the West Orange Public Works has been satisfied with past performance of this  vendor, who is agreeable to existing rates to be charged, at $240/hr. for two large trucks and $210/hr.  for a small truck; and  WHEREAS,   by Resolution No. 190-17 dated August 15, 2017 the Township Council authorized  the extension for a one (1) year for the year(s) 2017-2018; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Engineer recommends extension of this contract for a third year, and  the Township Council concurs with this recommendation. 246-18  October 30, 2018  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS , A Township Committee pursuant to the advertisement in the West Orange Chronicle  on Thursday, August 23, 2018 received three bids on Thursday, September 13, 2018 for the project  entitled "Roof and Exterior Improvements at Fire Stations No. 3, 4 and 5, West Orange, New Jersey"; and  WHEREAS,   the bids of the three contractors are as follows:  1.   Complete Roof Systems  25 Aladdin Avenue  Dumont, New Jersey 07628  Base Bid:   $482,005.00  Alt. Bid No. 1  Clean & Paint Radio Tower: No Bid   0  Total   $482, 005.00*  2.   Integrity Roofing  1385 Witherspoon Street  Rahway, New Jersey 07065  Base Bid:   $596,340.00  Alt. Bid No. 1  Clean & Paint Radio Tower:   $40,000.00  Total   $636,340.00  3.   Northeast Roof Maintenance  649 Catherine Street  Perth Amboy, New Jersey 08861  Base Bid:   $648,000.00  Alt. No. 1  Clean & Paint Radio Tower   $415,000.00  Total   $1,063,000.00;   and  WHEREAS,   the "No Bid" for the Alternate Bid 1 by Complete Roof Systems renders that bid non-  responsive; and  WHEREAS,   the funds available for the Improvements at the three Fire Houses totals  approximately $390,000; and

WHEREAS,   by letter dated October 24, 2018 the Municipal Engineer recommends all bids be  rejected due to insufficient funds and that the work be divided into two separate projects   –   Roof  Improvements and Exterior Improvements to promote competition amongst contractors of the distinct  trades and to enable a contract for roof improvements to be awarded given the funds available; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs in the foregoing.  NOW, THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange  that all bids for the project entitled "Roof and Exterior Improvements for Fire Stations No. 3, 4 and 5,  West Orange, New Jersey" be rejected and that the bid of Complete Roof Systems of Dumont, New  Jersey be considered non-responsive.  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that the project is authorized for rebid but as two distinct projects for  roof improvements and for exterior improvements subject to the Township Council authorizing an  additional appropriation in the amount of $3,920.00 with the architectural firm of Herbst-Musciano of  Boonton, New Jersey for the costs of preparing two separate sets of plans, specifications and bid  documents.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Susan McCartney, Council President  Municipal Clerk  Adopted: October 30, 2018

247-18  October 30, 2018  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS,   the Township Council of the West Orange by Resolution No. 112-18 dated April 24,  2018 authorized a professional services contract with the architectural firm of Herbst-Musciano, 611  Main Street, Boonton, New Jersey 07005 in the amount of $111,938.00 to design improvements for Fire  Stations No. 3, 4 and 5 pursuant to their proposal dated April 12, 2018; and  WHEREAS,   Herbst-Muscano prepared plans specifications and bid documents entitled "Roof and  Exterior Improvements for Fire Stations No. 3, 4 and 5, West Orange New Jersey" dated August 27,  2018; and  WHEREAS,   three bids were received on Thursday, September 13, 2018 which all exceeded the  funds available; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer by letter dated October 24, 2018 recommended the Council  reject all bids and the work be rebid as two separate projects for roof improvements and exterior  improvements; and  WHEREAS,   in rebidding the work as two projects the Township will incur additional professional  service expenses from Herbst-Musciano in the amount of $3,920.00 as stated in their proposal of  October 22, 2018; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Engineer finds the proposal acceptable; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs in the foregoing,  NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange  that an additional appropriation of $3,920.00 be authorized for the architectural firm Herbst-Musciano,  611 Main Street, Boonton, N.J. 07005 for additional services associated with preparing two separate  costs of plans, specifications and bid documents for Roof Improvements and Exterior Improvements at  Fire Stations No. 3, 4 and 5.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C ., Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney, Council President  Adopted: October 30, 2018  I hereby certify funds available from:   Account No. 03-2507-17-0100-010  Indoor Facilities, Fire Buildings  $3,920.00  John O. pages.text: 249-18  October 30, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   Wings   Air   Helicopter   ( “ Wings ” )   and   the   Essex   County   Country   Club   (the  “ ECCC ” )   have   requested   consent   from   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   to   land   a  helicopter   on   its   property   at   350   Mt.   Pleasant   Avenue   for   a   private   event   on   November   17,   2018 ,  see annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A; ”   and  WHERAS,   Wings   and   the   ECCC   must   apply   to   the   New   Jersey   Department   of  Transportation   (the   “ DOT ” )   for   a   Temporary   Aeronautical   Facility   License   (a   “ Temporary  License ” )   to land the helicopter at the ECCC property ; and  WHERAS,   the   application   to   the   DOT   requires   a   “ letter,   statement,   or   certificate   from  the   appropriate   governing   body   which   states   there   is   no   objection   to   the   issuance   of   a   temporary  license (mandatory for all temps) ; ”   and  WHERAS,   the   appropriate   body   is   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange   it   the   appropriate   body   to   provide   consent   or   “ no   objection ”   to   the   issuance   of   a  Temporary License;   and  WHERAS,   Wings   has   provided   to   the   Township   an   Certificate   of   Insurance   which  names the Township as an additional insured , see annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ B; ”   and  WHERAS,   the   appropriate   notice   shall   be   provided   to   West   Orange   Police   Department ’ s  Central Communications Center as to the date and time of the event.  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   that   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange ,   does   and   hereby   states   that   it   has   no   objection   to   the   issuance   of   a   Temporary  License   to   the   ECCC ,   located   at   350   Mt.   Pleasant   Avenue ,   West   Orange,   NJ   07052,   for   the   even  to be held on November 17, 2018 ; and be it further

RESOLVED,   that   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   for  reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. Carnevale , R.M.C.
