pages.text: 221-18  September 25, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Open   Space   and   Recreation   Committee   (the   “ Open  Space   Committee ” )   was   established   by   ordinance   to   perform   functions,   including:   (1)   studying   the   costs,  benefits   and   manner   of   acquiring,   maintaining   and   protecting   open   space   within   the   Township's   borders,  report   to   and   make   recommendations   to   the   Mayor   and   Council   regarding   its   findings   on   at   least   an  annual   basis;   and   (2)   prepare,   maintain   and   periodically   update   an   inventory   of   undeveloped   land   within  the   Township,   whether   publicly   or   privately   owned,   and   shall   make   such   inventory   available   to   the  Township's Mayor and Council ;   and  WHEREAS ,   in   order   to   perform   these   function,   the   Open   Space   Committee   seeks   to   prepare   an  update   to   the   Open   Space   and   Recreation   Plan   (the   “ Plan ” )   for   submission   to   the   Mayor   and   Township  Council ;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   Open   Space   Committee   seeks   to   retain   the   Land   Conservancy   of   New   Jersey,  located at 19 Boonton Avenue in Boonton, New Jersey 07005 (the   “ Land Conservancy ” ) ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Land   Conservancy ’ s   qualifications   and   experience   is   set   forth   in   the  Qualification page of its   proposal ,   annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A; ”   and  WHEREAS,   the   Land   Conservancy   has   agreed   to   assist   the   Open   Space   Committee   for   a   fee   of  $10,000   pursuant   to   the   terms   and   provisions   of   the   proposed   agreement,   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ B ”  (the   “ Agreement ” ); and  WHEREAS,   the   price   set   forth   in   the   Agreement   falls   below   the   statutory   bid   threshold,  exempting the Contract for public bidding.  NOW ,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF  WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   is   authorized   to   retain   the   Land   Conservancy   to   assist   the   Open  Space Commission with the preparation of the Plan; and it is further ;  RESOLVED ,   that   the   Mayor   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the   Agreement,   annexed   hereto   as  Exhibit   “ B ”   and that the Township Clerk is authorized to attest to the Mayor ’ s signature;   and be it further

RESOLVED ,   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   to   the  extent required with applicable law .  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C., Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney, Council President  Adopted: September 25, 2018  I hereby certify funds are available from:  Account No.  John O. Gross, Chief Financial Officer  4814-8667-3523, v. 1
Exhibit   “A”

QUALIFICATIONS  The Land Conservancy is uniquely experienced and well qualified to assist West Orange Township with the completion of the update to its 2009 Open Space and Recreation Plan (OSRP). We are a fully comprehensive planning organization with staff skilled and experienced in the requirements and analyses needed to prepare Open Space Plans. We have the capacity and technical ability to quickly and efficiently step in to complete these reports without delay.  We completed West Orange Township’s 2002 Open Space and Recreation Plan and updated this report in 2004 and 2009. Working closely with the Township and Essex County Executive Joseph N. DiVincenzo, Jr., Essex County Department of Parks, Recreation and Cultural Affairs, the South Mountain Conservancy, we purchased and preserved 11 acres in 2009 which has since been added to South Mountain Reservation in West Orange as part of Mayapple Hill.  Our staff has completed 80 Open Space Plans for municipalities throughout New Jersey. Each of these communities received certification through Sustainable Jersey and funding through the New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection Green Acres program through submittal of these reports, which are required to be updated every ten years.  The resources of our office include a state-of-the-art Geographic Information Systems (GIS) center with large scale plotter and numerous high-quality laser printers capable of printing smaller maps for handouts and publication. The Land Conservancy has licensed software which includes ArcGIS 10.6, PhotoShop, InDesign, Adobe Professional, and the Microsoft Office Suite. Staff is skilled at a variety of web-based software.  The Land Conservancy of New Jersey is a nonprofit land trust organization dedicated to permanently protecting land for conservation of natural resources, recreation, and water quality protection. Founded in 1981, we have helped 100 government and private organizations preserve, steward, and protect open space. We have written 142 plans, preserved 25,000 acres, and helped towns receive $239 million for land preservation. Our staff includes a licensed planner, technical writers, skilled GIS cartographers, naturalists, and graduate students from the Bloustein School of Urban Planning at Rutgers University. The Land Conservancy has received numerous awards for our work:  •   2017 – NJDEP GIS – 1 st   Place Best Analytical Presentation – Sussex County Open Space Plan Update  •   2016 – New Jersey BIZ – Public Private Partnership Award – Bergen County Flood Planning  •   2015 – New Jersey Planning Officials Achievement in Planning – Preservation of Beaver Brook Farm presented to Warren County, in partnership with The Land Conservancy of New Jersey.  •   2015 – New Jersey Chapter of the American Water Resources Association Excellence in Water Resources Protection and Planning Award for the South Branch Preserve Ecological Restoration Project in Mt. Olive Township, Morris County.

TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE:   O PEN   S PACE AND   R ECREATION   P LAN   U PDATE  S COPE OF   WORK  The Land Conservancy of New Jersey will help West Orange Township with the following activities:  An   Open Space and Recreation Plan   is a comprehensive document that focuses and guides local open space preservation and land management programs. The Land Conservancy of New Jersey follows the NJDEP Green Acres program requirements for format and content for a municipal Open Space and Recreation Plan (OSRP) to ensure the municipality will qualify for state funding for land acquisition. Green Acres requires that an OSRP must have been re-examined within the past 10 years and adopted as an amendment to the Master Plan. West Orange Township’s OSRP has enabled the Township to access funding through the Green Acres program. Updating the report will incorporate the current open space objectives of the municipality, including conservation and stewardship projects.  The   Open Space and Recreation Plan Update   includes the following sections:  1.   Open Space Goals and objectives of the community are evaluated and updated  2.   Inventory of the currently protected open space and undeveloped lands  3.   History of the Open Space Program including expenditures made and grants received  4.   Planning Review of new and updated state, county, and local plans for program information  5.   Maps using ArcGIS to determine priority lands for preservation and restoration  6.   Preservation Recommendations & Action program for open space protection over a period of five years  All maps in the document will be updated to reflect current tax assessment data. Maps are on an ArcGIS 10.6 mapping platform. This software allows the environmental data to be layered and visualized with greater diversity and accuracy.  As part of our service, we will facilitate up to two (2) public meetings as required by NJDEP Green Acres for the Plan Update. Staff will attend up to four (4) meetings in the Township, including the public meetings. To meet Green Acres standards the Plan is required to be adopted as a part of the Master Plan.  Deliverables  The Land Conservancy can start the Plan immediately and a draft will be ready for review within four to six months. All maps, figures and tables in the document will be updated to incorporate the most current data. If the Township requires other information to be included in the Plan, above what is within this proposal, the costs may change. The OSRP Update will be finalized based upon comments received.The Land Conservancy will deliver one hard copy of the final report. An electronic copy of the draft and final reports will be sent via an internet link so it may be downloaded directly to the user’s computer.  The Land Conservancy of New Jersey will research the feasibility of developing online web-based mapping as a companion to the Township’s Open Space and Recreation Plan Update. If successful, we will share this with the Township for use by the volunteers and professionals as part of the open space program. If approved by the governing body, The Land Conservancy will provide the data to the municipality’s technical service provider to upload this data to the Township’ss website.  F EE  $10,000  Proposal submitted June 25, 2018  Work to commence upon approval and authorization by West Orange Township
Exhibit   “B”

A G R E E M E N T  BY AND BETWEEN  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE, County of Essex  A Municipal Corporation of the State of  New Jersey, with offices located at  66 Main Street  West Orange, New Jersey 07052  Hereinafter referred to as “ Township”  AND  THE LAND CONSERVANCY OF NEW JERSEY  Open Space and Farmland Preservation Advisors  with offices located at  19 Boonton Avenue  Boonton, New Jersey 07005  Hereinafter referred to as “ Conservancy”  W I T N E S S E T H:  WHEREAS , with the identification of lands for natural   resource, recreation,   and   cultural protection, a need exists to retain the services of an open space preservation consultant whose staff members have technical expertise and experience in the field of open space preservation and stewardship; and  WHEREAS , The Land Conservancy of New Jersey is a nonprofit organization whose mission is to preserve and protect open space for natural, historic, agricultural and recreational purposes and to assist municipalities in the stewardship of open space lands; and  WHEREAS , the Township of West Orange desires to develop an updated Open Space and Recreation Plan (“Plan”) and  WHEREAS , The Land Conservancy of New Jersey is qualified to prepare an updated Open Space and Recreation Plan;

NOW, THEREFORE,   it is mutually agreed by and between the parties as follows:  1.   Scope of Services:   The Land Conservancy of New Jersey shall provide the Township of West Orange, Essex County, with the services as outlined in Schedule A.  2.   Maps:   Maps will be developed for this project, as determined by The Land Conservancy, in consultation with the Township, and described in Schedule A. The maps will be formatted to be 8 ½ x 11 inches in size.  3.   Meeting Attendance:   The Land Conservancy may attend up to four (4) meetings in the Township of West Orange as part of this project, as determined by The Land Conservancy, in consultation with the Township. If the Township requests attendance at additional municipal-based meetings, The Land Conservancy reserves the right to charge a fee on top of the currently approved fee of $125 per hour, plus cost of travel.  4.   Schedule:   The term of the agreement shall be for a twelve (12) month period commencing on October 1, 2018 and ending on September 30, 2019.   The Land Conservancy will present the Township with the draft Plan Update by March 1, 2019. The Land Conservancy will deliver a final draft of the Plan Update to the Township by September 30, 2019, dependent upon municipal review and comments. In order to complete the Plan by this date, The Land Conservancy needs to receive the informational materials and documents, as identified by The Land Conservancy from the Township, no later than November 30, 2018. If needed, Township representatives and municipal officials involved in the development of the Plan will meet six (6) weeks prior to the delivery of the final version of the Plan to determine if there are any deficiencies due to the lack of information requested by The Land Conservancy but not delivered from the Township.  5.   Deliverables:   The Plan will be produced using the format created by The Land Conservancy for these documents. The Land Conservancy will complete one (1) Draft for Township review. The Township will provide its comments to The Land Conservancy on the Draft report.   Following review by the Township boards, committees and staff, one (1) Revised Draft will be produced for review by the Planning Board. Upon approval by the Planning Board a Final Plan Update will be issued.   No further draft reports will be produced, if the Township requests an additional draft reports it will be completed at an agreed upon fee between the Township and The Land Conservancy.   Upon completion of the final report, The Land Conservancy will provide the Township one (1) hard copy, in color, of the final version. An electronic copy of the Draft and Final reports will be sent via an internet link so it may be downloaded directly to the user’s computer.  6.   Delivery of Final Product:   Upon delivery of the Final report to the Township, The Land Conservancy will include a cover letter stating that the report is now complete.

7.   Term of   Agreement:   The term of the agreement shall be for a twelve (12) month period commencing on October 1, 2018 and ending on September 30, 2019.  8.   Total Compensation:   Payment shall be made by the Borough to The Land Conservancy for a fee of ten thousand dollars ($10,000.00).   Payments will be processed through the Township Committee as follows:  •   Attendance at Kick-Off meeting……………………………...…… $2,000  •   Completion of draft Open Space Map ……………………..……... $2,000  •   Submittal of Draft Plan Update………………..…………………... $2,000  •   Submittal of Final Plan Update ……………….………………..…..   $4,000  The Land Conservancy is able to keep the costs for these services low thanks to a generous grant from the Geraldine R. Dodge Foundation which underwrites this program.  9.   Payment Procedures:   The Land Conservancy shall be responsible in submitting to the Chief Financial Officer a voucher with specific charges. Method of payment shall comply with the Local Fiscal Affairs Law, N.J.S.A. 40A:5-16, which prohibits the governing body of any local unit from paying out any of its monies “unless the person claiming or receiving the same shall first present a detailed bill of items or demand, specifying particularly how the bill or demand is made up, with the certification of the party claiming payment that it is correct.”  10.   Ownership of Records:   All records, maps, plans and data pertaining to any Township open space issue and any other information utilized or gathered by The Land Conservancy on behalf of the Township shall be surrendered to the Township upon expiration of this Agreement.   The Land Conservancy may retain copies as it determines and will not disclose any information known to it to be non-public.  11.   Work Product:   All material and work product, researched, created and pursuant to this agreement belong solely to The Land Conservancy.   The text of the report may not be modified by the Township or anyone on its behalf without the prior written consent of The Land Conservancy. If requested by the Township, The Land Conservancy will include the ArcGIS shape files for the data used to create the maps, upon completion of the final report.  12.   Cancellation of Agreement:   Both the Townsip and The Land Conservancy reserve the right to cancel this Agreement without reason upon thirty (30) calendar days written notice. However, both parties agree that any pending business will be completed to the best of The Land Conservancy’s abilities, and that an orderly transition of the project will be made in cooperation with the Township Clerk.  13.   Mandatory Affirmative Action Compliance.   During the term of this agreement, the parties agree to comply with the Affirmative Action requirements set forth in N.J.A.C. 17:27, and hereby

incorporate by reference the mandatory Affirmative Action language set forth in Exhibit “A” which is attached and made a part of this Agreement.  14.   Arbitration .   This Agreement is the entire agreement of the parties on the subject matter, superseding all prior written or oral understandings or agreements, and may be amended only by a writing signed by both parties. All disputes in respect of this Agreement not settled by negotiation within 30 days of written notice thereof by either party to the other shall be submitted by one party or the other to non-binding mediation by a mutually agreed upon mediator and if not settled within 60 days of such notice shall be finally resolved by confidential binding arbitration in Morristown, New Jersey under the rules then in effect of the American Arbitration Association (other than its Administrative Rules) by a panel of three arbitrators, one selected by each party and the third by the two so selected. Any decision by a majority or more of the arbitrators may be entered as a judgment in any court having jurisdiction. The arbitrators shall have no authority to amend this Agreement. One-half of the expenses of the mediator of any mediation and the arbitrators in any arbitration shall be borne by each party.  IN WITNESS THEREOF , the parties hereto have executed this agreement on this day of   2018.  ATTEST:   TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  ___________________________   By__________________________  Borough Clerk   Mayor  WITNESS:   THE LAND CONSERVANCY OF NEW JERSEY  ___________________________   By__________________________  David J. Epstein, President

EXHIBIT A  MANDATORY EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY LANGUAGE  N.J.S.A. 10:5-31 et seq., N.J.A.C. 17:27  GOODS, PROFESSIONAL SERVICES AND GENERAL SERVICE CONTRACTS  During the performance of this contract, the contractor agrees as follows:  The contractor or subcontractor, where applicable, will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of age, race, creed, color, national origin, ancestry, marital status, affectional or sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, nationality or sex. Except with respect to affectional or sexual orientation and gender identity or expression, the contractor will ensure that equal employment opportunity is afforded to such applicants in recruitment and employment, and that employees are treated during employment, without regard to their age, race, creed, color, national origin, ancestry, marital status, affectional or sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, nationality or sex. Such equal employment opportunity shall include, but not be limited to the following: employment, upgrading, demotion, or transfer; recruitment or recruitment advertising; layoff or termination; rates of pay or other forms of compensation; and selection for training, including apprenticeship. The contractor agrees to post in conspicuous places, available to employees and applicants for employment, notices to be   provided   by   the   Public   Agency   Compliance   Officer   setting   forth   provisions   of   this nondiscrimination clause.  The contractor or subcontractor, where applicable will, in all solicitations or advertisements for employees placed by or on behalf of the contractor, state that all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, creed, color, national origin, ancestry, marital   status,   affectional   or   sexual   orientation,   gender   identity   or   expression,   disability, nationality or sex.  The contractor or subcontractor will send to each labor union, with which it has a collective bargaining agreement, a notice, to be provided by the agency contracting officer, advising the labor union of the contractor's commitments under this chapter and shall post copies of the notice in conspicuous places available to employees and applicants for employment.  The contractor or subcontractor where applicable, agrees to comply with any regulations promulgated by the Treasurer pursuant to   N.J.S.A. 10:5-31 et seq.   as amended and supplemented from time to time and the Americans with Disabilities Act.  The contractor or subcontractor agrees to make good faith efforts to meet targeted county employment goals established in accordance with   N.J.A.C. 17:27-5.2 .  The contractor or subcontractor agrees to inform in writing its appropriate recruitment agencies including, but not limited to, employment agencies, placement bureaus, colleges, universities, labor unions, that it does not discriminate on the basis of age, race, creed, color, national origin, ancestry, marital status, affectional or sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability,

nationality or sex, and that it will discontinue the use of any recruitment agency which engages in direct or indirect discriminatory practices.  The contractor or subcontractor agrees to revise any of its testing procedures, if necessary, to assure that all personal testing conforms with the principles of job-related testing, as established by the statutes and court decisions of the State of New Jersey and as established by applicable Federal law and applicable Federal court decisions.  In conforming with the targeted employment goals, the contractor or subcontractor agrees to review all procedures relating to transfer, upgrading, downgrading and layoff to ensure that all such actions are taken without regard to age, race, creed, color, national origin, ancestry, marital status, affectional or sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, nationality or sex, consistent with the statutes and court decisions of the State of New Jersey, and applicable Federal law and applicable Federal court decisions.  The contractor shall submit to the public agency, after notification of award but prior to execution of a goods and services contract, one of the following three documents:  Letter of Federal Affirmative Action Plan Approval  Certificate of Employee Information Report  Employee Information Report Form AA302 (electronically provided by the Division and distributed   to   the   public   agency   through   the   Division’s   website   at www.state.nj.us/treasury/contract_compliance)  The contractor and its subcontractors shall furnish such reports or other documents to the Division of Public Contracts Equal Employment Opportunity Compliance as may be requested by the office from time to time in order to carry out the purposes of these regulations, and public agencies shall furnish such information as may be requested by the Division of Public Contracts Equal Employment Opportunity Compliance for conducting a compliance investigation pursuant to  Subchapter 10 of the Administrative Code at N.J.A.C. 17:27. documents.title: RESOLUTION FOR NON FAIR AND OPEN WITH OMEGA ENVIRONMENTAL

pages.text: 222-18  September 25, 2018  RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING THE   EMERGENCY   AWARD OF   A NON-FAIR AND OPEN  CONTRACT   TO   O MEGA ENVIRONMENTAL SERVICES, INC.  WHEREAS, the   Township of West Orange   ha d   a need to   hire   in an emergency ,   Omega  Environmental, Inc.   an environmental health and safety company,   to do   a   Legionella   assessment and  water sampling a s a non-fair and open contract pursuant to the provisions   of N.J.S.A.   19:44A-20.5 ; and,  WHEREAS, the   Purchasing Agent has determined that the cost of these services will be over  17,500.00; and  WHEREAS, the Township took a   proactive position after learning an employee had contracted  Legionnaires   disease. Omega Environmental was immediately hired for   the testing and   assessment of  eleven locations; and  WHEREAS, these assessments required   emergency contracts   without quote with   the Nephros  Company to install infectious control filters on all water lines and Rocco Ciufalo Plumbing and Heating  to   assist   with filter installation and various related work   and remediation;  WHEREAS,   Omega Environmental, Inc.   has completed and submitted a Business Entity  Disclosure Certification which certifies that   Omega Environmental, Inc. located at 280 Huyer Street,  South Hackensack, NJ 07606   has not made any reportable contributions to a political or candidate  committee in the   Township of West Orange in the previous year and that contributions are prohibited  through the term of the contract; and  WHEREAS,   the cost of these services   with Omega Environmental, Inc.   are $41,549.00 and   the  Township Chief Financial Officer has   certified there are sufficient and available funds   as required by  N.J.A.C. 5:30-5.4 .  NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED that the   Township Council of the Township of West  Orange   authorizes the   services of Omega Environmental, Inc. as described herein.  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the Business Disclosure Entity   Certification be   placed on file  with this resolution.  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that   the Clerk shall publish such notice as required by law. This  appointment was made without competitive bidding as professional services   contract   under the provision  of the Local Public Contracts Law, as provided in N.J.S.A. 40A:11-5 .  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: September 25, 2018  I here certify funds available:   Account # 03-2507-17-0100-020 - Amount $41,549.00  John O. Gross, Chief Financial Officer

223 - 18  September 25, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   in 2017 Block 107, Lot 1.04, ASI inadvertently put an added a ssessment on  a non - existent lot number ; and  WHEREAS ,   Block 107, Lot 1.04   is   on the books   in the duplicate resulting in a n added  assessment   for a non - existing lot ;  WHEREAS , the Tax Col lector will cancel the added assessment ; and  NOW ,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT,   AND   HEREBY   IT   IS,   RESOLVED   BY   THE  TOWNSHIP COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE , that the tax assessor  and tax colle ctor   be and they are hereby auth orized , empowered and directed   to   canc el the   added  assessment   on Block 107, Lot 1.04   for the year   2017.  Karen Carnevale,   R.M.C.,   Municipal Clerk   Susan McCartney, Council President  Adopted:   September 25, 20 18

2550-18  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING   CHAPTER 7,  TRAFFIC, SUB SECTION 7-20, STOP INTERSECTIONS,   OF   THE REVISED  GENERAL ORDINANCES OF   THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  BE IT ORDAINED, by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange,  in the County of E ssex, State of New Jersey, that   Overlook   Avenue and   Ridgeview  Avenue   and Ferris Drive and Stanford Avenue   shall be designated as   Stop Intersection s  pursuant to N.J.S.A. 39:4-8(b) under the Municipal Engineer ’ s Certification and License  Number GE026706 as follows:  Section 1.   The Township Council of the Township of West Orange finds it in  the interest of public s afety to des i gnate   Overlook   Avenue   and   Ridgeview   Avenue   and  Ferris Drive and Stanford Avenue   as   Stop Intersection s   pursuant to N.J.S.A.39:4-8(b).  Section 2.   The Municipal Engineer of the Township of West Orange has  submitted and certified all the legislative requirements pursuant to N.J.S.A. 39:4-8(b) and  the criteria as set forth by the New Jersey Department of Transportation, Bureau of  Traffic Eng ineering and Investigations has   been met.  Section 3.   Subsection 7-20, Stop Intersections, of the Revised General  Ordinances of the Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as  follows pursuant to the provisions of N.J.S.A. 39:4-140 ( italic type denotes deletion,   bold  type denotes addition) :  Intersection   Stop Sign   On:  O verlook Avenue   Ridgeview Avenue  and Ridgeview Avenue  Ferris Drive   Stanford Avenue  and Stanford Avenue

Section 4.   If any provisions of this Ordinance or application thereof, under  any circumstances, is held invalid, the invalidity shall not affect any of the provisions or  applications of this Ordinance that can be given effect without the valid provision(s) or  application and to this end, the provisions of the Ordinance are severable.  Section 5.   All other Ordinances or parts of Ordinances inconsistent with this  Ordinance are hereby repealed.  Section 6.   Unless another penalty is expressly provided by New Jersey  Statute, every person convicted of a violation of any provision of this Ordinance or any  supplement thereto shall be liable to the penalty of not more that fifty dollars ($50.00) or  imprisonment for a term not to e xceed fifteen (15) days or both, as per W.O.R.G.O .  Chapter 7-4.2  ROBERT D. PARISI , MAYOR  SUSAN McCARTNEY , COUNCIL PRESIDENT  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, R.M.C.  MUNICIPAL CLERK  Introduced: September 4, 2018  Adopted: September 25, 2018

2550-18  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  The Ordinance, which amends the Traffic Regulations of the Township,  designates the intersection s   of Overlook Avenue a nd   Ridgeview   Avenue   and Ferris  Avenue and Stanford Avenue   as   Stop Intersection s   with   a   Stop Sign   to be placed on  Ridgeview   Avenue , the minor street , and a stop sign to be placed on Stanford Avenue,  the minor street.   T he   intersection s are both   tee   intersection s .   Due to volume of traffi c  wh ich travels on   Overlook   Avenue   and Ferris Drive   the Township ’ s Engineering  Division determined that traffic   needed to be regulated at th e   intersection s   for the safe  movement of traf fic.   Without   Stop Sign s   on   Ridgeview   Avenue   and Stanford Avenue ,   the  normal right-of-way rule is not expected to provide   a reasonably safe operation of th e s e  intersection s .

2551-18  A N ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING   CHAPTER 7, TRAFFIC, SUBSECTION 7-32.2,  RESTRICTED PARKING ZONES,   OF THE REVISED GEN E RAL ORDI N ANCES  OF   THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  BE IT ORDAINED,   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, in the County of  Essex, State of New Jersey as follows:  Section 1.   Subsection 7-32.2, Restricted Parking Zones, of the Revised General Ordinances  of the Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows ( italic type denotes  deletion,   bold type denotes addition):  7-32.2   Restricted Parking Zones. Pursuant to N.J.S.A 39:4-197.6, Restricted Parking Zones, as  defined in N.J.S.A. 39:4-207.6e, are established in front of the following residences as indicated on the  Tax Maps of the Township of West Orange, at the specified locations. Parking within a specific zone is  restricted to the motor vehicle issued a permit pursuant to N.J.S.A. 39:4-197.7. In accordance with  N.J.S.A., 39:4-197.6 parking is permitted in the Restricted Parking Zone except when prohibited in front  of the residence by other sections of this Chapter.  Residence Address   No. of Spaces   Location  31 Watson Avenue   1   Beginning   340   feet south of the  sou th erl y c urbline of Samuel Street  exte nded   and extending 20 feet south.  89 Ashland Avenue   1   Beginning 362 feet south   of the  southerly curbline of Park Avenue  extended and extending 19   feet  south.  9 Cross Street   1   Beginning 109   feet south of the  southerly curbline of Hazel   Avenue  ex tended and extending 19   feet sou th.

Section 2.   If any provision of this Ordinance or application thereof, under any  circumstances, is held invalid, the invalidity shall not affect any other provisions or applications of this  Ordinance that can be given effect without the invalid provision(s) or application(s) and to this end the  provisions of this Ordinance are severable.  Section 3.   All other Ordinances or parts of Ordinances inconsistent with this Ordinance are  hereby repealed.  Section 4.   This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication in  accordance with law and upon the approval of the New Jersey Department of Transportation if required.  ROBERT D. 2551-18  L EGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordi nance is in response to   request s   from   handicappe d residen t s   who   reside   at 31 Watson  Avenue, 89 Ashland Avenue, and 9 Cross Street .   By adopting this Ordinance, the Township will establish  a handicapped par king stall adjacent to   each of   these   address es . Such parking stalls are permissible  under the laws of New Jersey, and they are designated as Restricted Parking Zones.

2552-18 AN ORDINANCE CREATING A RECLASSIFICATION AND SALARY SCHEDULE OF CERTAIN TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE EMPLOYEES SETTING FORTH THEIR TITLES, CLASSIFICATION AND SALARY RANGES AND ADOPTING A SALARY GUIDE AND ESTABLISHING THE EMPLOYEE'S STATUS AS A TOWNSHIP EMPLOYEE AND UNDER THE GUIDELINES OF THE NEW JERSEY DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL. BE IT ORDAINED by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange as follows: SECTION 1. That: an ordinance adopting and creating the reclassification and salary schedules of certain Township of West Orange Municipal employees, setting a salary guide and establishing the employee's status as a Township employee and under the guidelines of the New Jersey Department of Personnel as filed in the office of the Municipal Clerk for the following positions: SEE   APPENDIX 1 - SALARY RANGES - NON UNIFORM TITLES - AFSCME SEE   APPENDIX 2 - SALARY RANGES - NON UNIFORM TITLES - MANAGEMENT - EXEMPT EMPLOYEES SEE   APPENDIX 3 - SALARY RANGES - NON UNIFORM TITLES - TEAMSTER SUPERVISORS SEE   APPENDIX 4 - SALARY RANGES - NON UNIFORM TITLES - TEAMSTER COMMUNICATIONS SEE   APPENDIX 5 - SALARY RANGES - NON UNIFORM TITLES-HOURLY SEE   APPENDIX 6 - SALARY RANGES - STIPENDS SEE   APPENDIX 7 - SALARY RANGES - UNIFORM TITLES - FIRE SUPERIORS SEE   APPENDIX 8 - SALARY RANGES - UNIFORM TITLES - FIREFIGHTERS SEE   APPENDIX 9 - SALARY RANGES - UNIFORM TITLES - POLICE SUPERIORS SEE   APPENDIX 10 - SALARY RANGES - UNIFORM TITLES - POLICE SECTION 2. All terms and conditions included in any authorized collective bargaining agreements and/or employment contracts are hereby included in SECTION 1 above. SECTION 4. Part time employees have no annual increment for their hourly rate in Section 1 above. SECTION 5. This ordinance and the titles and salary ranges herein provided shall become effective on the dates specified and as indicated above in SECTION 1. Where the specified timeframes have expired, the salary range specified in the latest timeframe will remain in effect until a new reange is adopted by Ordinance. SECTION 6. All ordinances and parts of ordinances heretofore enacted which are inconsistent with any provision of this ordinance are, to the extent of such inconsistency, hereby repealed. SECTION 7. This ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication in accordance with the laws of the state of New Jersey. ___________________________________________ ROBERT D. PARISI, MAYOR ___________________________________________ SUSAN McCARTNEY, COUNCIL PRESIDENT Introduced: September 25, 2018 Adopted: October 16, 2018 Approved as to form and legality on the basis of the facts set forth. ______________________________________________ Karen J. Carnevale, R.M. C., Municipal Clerk SECTION 3. All part time employee hourly rates are determined by dividing the Minimum and Maximum rates by the # of annual hours a full time employee would work for that position using the salaries identified in Section 1 above.

2553-18  September 25, 2018  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING  CHAPTER 7, TRAFFIC, SUBSECTION 7- 29 .1,   SPEED LIMITS IN GENERAL,  AND SUBSECTION 7-33, SCHOOL ZONES,  OF THE REVISED GENERAL ORDINANCES OF  THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  BE IT ORDAINED,   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, in the County of  Essex, State of New Jersey as follows:  Section 1.   Subsection 7- 29 .1 ,   Speed Limits in General,   of   the Revised General Ordinances  of the Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows ( italic type denotes  deletion,   bold type denotes addition):  Name of Street   Direction   Speed Limit   Location  Pleasant Valley Way   Both   40 MPH   40 MPH from the center of N.J.  State Highway   Route   No.   10  (Mt.   P leasant Ave.) to  325   feet   south of center of  Greenwood Avenue, except for  a School Speed Limit of 25  MPH from   100 feet north of  the   center of Cecil Lane Place  to the northerly limits of this  speed zone as described above  while children are going to and  from school during opening  and closing hours.  Pleasant Valley Way   Both   30 MPH   30 MPH from 325   feet   south of  the   c enter of Greenwood  Avenue   to the center of  Marmon Terrace   –   Kenz  Terrace except   for a school  speed limit of 25 MPH from  100 feet north of the center  of Greenwood Avenue to the  southerly limits of this speed  zone as described above   while  children are going to and from  school during opening and  closing hours .  Pleasant Valley Way   Both   40 MPH   40 MPH from the center of  Marmon Terrace   –   Kenz  Terrace to   the   Municipal  Boundary   with Verona.

Section 2.   Subsection 7-33, School Zones, of the Revised General Ordinance of the  Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows ( italic type denotes deletion,  bold type denotes addition):  School   Street   Location  West Orange High School/   Pleasant Valley Way   From Alisa Drive  Pleasantdale School   inclusive of the intersection  to a point 200 feet south of  the southerly curbline of  Greenwood Avenue.  West Orange High School /   Pleasant Valley Way   From 100 feet north  Kelly (Pleasantdale)   of the center of Cecil  Elementary School   Lane Place to 100 feet  north of the center of  Greenwood Avenue.  Section   3 .   If any provision s   of this Ordinance or application thereof, under any  circumstances,   is held invalid, the invalidity shall not   affect   any other provisions or applications of this  Ordinance that can be given effect without the invalid provision(s) or application(s) and to this end the  provisions of this Ordinance are severable.  Section   4 .   All other Ordinances or parts of Ordinances inconsistent with this Ordinance are  hereby repealed.  Section   5 .   This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication in  accordance with law   and upon the approval by Resolution of the Essex County Board of Chosen  Freeholders .  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor   Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C., Municipal Clerk  Susan McCartney, Council President  Introduced: September 25, 2018  Adopted: October 16, 2018

2553-18  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance amends the limits of the School Speed Zone established by the Essex County  Board of Chosen Freeholders ’   Resolution No. 24246 adopted September 24, 1964. The Ordinance also  codifies in the West Orange Revised General Ordinances the speed limits established for Pleasant Valley  Way in West Orange from the center of State Highway No. 10 to the Verona line by that same  Freeholder Resolution. This Ordinances amends Ordinance No. 2488-16 by deleting the speed limits of  50 miles per hour and 45 miles per hour in both directions between Northfield Avenue and the center of  State Highway No. 10. These speed limits were reduced to 40 miles per hour by Ordinance No. 2534-17.  This Ordinance establishes the southerly limits of the School Speed Zone as 100 feet north of the center  of Cecil Lane Place alongside Degnan Park, and it establishes the northerly limits of the school speed  zone 100 feet north of the center of Greenwood Avenue alongside the PNC Bank. The previous limits  were 570 feet north of the center of Lakeview Drive (the southerly limit) alongside Lincoln Field and 325  feet south of the center of Greenwood Avenue (the northerly limit) at the beginning of the Pleasantdale  School.  This Ordinance also amends the limits of the School Zone on Pleasant Valley Way for the West  Orange High School and the Kelly (formerly Pleasantdale) Elementary School to coincide with the School  Speed Zone Limits.

2554-18  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING CHAPTER   4 , SECTION   1.3   OF THE REVISED  GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  ( Sweeping Litter, Garbage, Refuse or Rubbish into Gutter Prohibited. )  BE IT ORDAINED BY THE MUNICIPAL COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST  ORANGE, NEW JERSEY   that Chapter   4   of the Revised General Ordinances of the Township  of West Orange be and are hereby amended as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  The purpose of this ordinance is to   revise   the municipal code provision governing the  sweeping of litter, garbage, refuse or rubbish   within the Township of West Orange   to include the  addition of grass.  II.   CHAPTER   4,   SECTION   1.3   SHALL   BE   AND   HEREBY   IS   AMENDED   AND  SUPPLEMENTED   TO INCORPORATE THE FOLLOWING :  4-1.3   Sweeping Litter, Garbage,   Grass,   Refuse or Rubbish into Gutter Prohibited.  No   person,   including   merchants   owning   or   occupying   a   place   of   business,   shall   sweep  into   or   deposit   in   any   gutter   or   other   public   place   within   the   Township   the   accumulation   of   litter,  garbage,   grass,   refuse   or   rubbish   from   any   block   or   lot   or   from   any   public   or   private   sidewalk   or  driveway.   Persons   owning   or   occupying   property   shall   keep   the   sidewalk   in   front   of   their  premises free of litter, garbage, grass, refuse and rubbish.  I II .   REPEAL OF CONFLICTING ORDINANCES  Any   Ordinances   of   the   Township   which   are   in   conflict   with   this   Ordinance   are   hereby  repealed to the extent of such conflict.  I V .   SEVERABILITY  If   any   part   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   deemed   invalid,   such   parts   shall   be   severed   and   th e  invalidity thereof shall not a ffect the remaining parts of this Ordinance.  V .   EFFECTIVE DATE  This   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   final   passage   and   publication   in   accordance   with  the law.  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor   Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C., Municipal Clerk  Susan McCartney, Council President  Introduced: September 25, 2018  Adopted: October 16, 2018

documents.title: EXHIBIT - FIRST AMENDMENT TO INTERIM REDEVELOPMENT AGREEMENT BETWEEN TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE AND THE ALPERT GROUP LLC

pages.text: 192-18  September 4, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   on   March   7,   2017,   the   Township   Council   adopted   Resolution   No.  74- 17   which   authorized   the   Mayor   to   execute   an   Inter im   Redeve lo p ment   Agreement   with  T he Alpert Group, LLC ( “ Alpert ” ) ; and  WHEREAS,   on   March   7,   2017,   the   Township   and   Alpert   executed   the   Interim  Redevelopment Agreement ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Interim   Redevelopment   Agreement   remains   in   full   force   and  effect ;   and  WHEREAS,   part   of   the   designated   areas   i ncludes   Lot   32,   Block   9   on   the   Tax  M ap   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   which   is   also   known   as   the   Biddleman   Parcel   (the  “ Property ” ) ;   and  WHEREAS   Alpert has entered into a Contract to acquire the Property ;   and  WHEREAS   th e   Property   is   part   of   the   Brown f ields   Development   area   “ BDA ”  which   provides   a   source   of   funds   for   the   investigation   and   the   re mediation   of   certain  environmental issues ;   and  WHEREAS,   the   Property   is   directly   conti g uous   to   the   Selecto   Flash   Property  which   the   Township   currently   owns   and   which   Parcel   is   also   part   of   the   redevelopment  area ;   and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   desires   to   support   the   redevelopment   area   by  acquiring   the   Property   for   a   potential   public   use   such   as   parking   or   other   comple mentary  uses   to the residential and commercial neighborhood; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   and   Alpert   have   negotiated   the   First   Amendment   to  Interim Redevelopment Agreement which was annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A ” ;   and

NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the  Township   of   West   Orange   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the  annexed   First   Amendment   to   Interim   Redevelopment   Agreement   between   t he   Township   of  West Orange and T he Alpert Group, LLC;  IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   notice   of   the   approval   and   execution   of   this  A greement   shall   be   made   in   accordance   with   l aw   and   a   copy   of   the   First   Amendment   shall  be maintained by the Municipal Clerk for the Township of West Orange.  Approval of this Resolution is acceptable to the Law  Department based upon the facts and circumstances  provided.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   September 4, 2018  4811-3786-6096, v. 1

1  193-18  September 4, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   Valley   Road   Residential   Urban   Renewal,   LLC   ( “ Valley   Road   Residential ” )  owns   certain   Property   in   the   Township   of   West   Orange   ( “ Township ” )   located   at   22   Central  Avenue   known   as   Block   9,   Lots   1,   7,   44,   50   and   56   which   consists   of   approximately   1.5   acres;  and  WHEREAS ,   the   tax   map   for   the   property   now   known   as   Block   9,   Lot   1.01   has   a   sketch  of a storm water division structure marked as a Storm Easement; and  WHEREAS ,   the   storm   water   drainage   channel   that   runs   along   the   east   side   of   the  property   has   been   fully   upgraded   in   accordance   with   approvals   and   permits   granted   by   all  applicable government agencies; and  WHEREAS , the storm water diversion structure has been abandoned and removed; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   engineer   has   confirmed   the   storm   water   division   structure   has  been abandoned; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   now   seeks   to   abandon,   terminate   and   release   any   easement  that   previously   may   have   existed   by   reason   of   the   sketch   on   its   tax   maps   and   that   the   tax   map   be  updated   and   revised   to   remove   any   such   sketch   on   the   property   now   known   as   Tax   Map   Block   9,  Lot 1.01 by entering into the Release of Storm Easement annexed hereto as   Exhibit   “ A; ”  NOW,   BEING   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Release   of   Storm   Easement   be   and   is   hereby  approved   and   Valley   Road   Residential   shall   have   the   right   to   have   the   Release   of   Storm  Easement recorded in the public land records; and it is further

2  RESOLVED ,   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the   Release   of  Storm   Easement   and   the   Municipal   Clerk   is   hereby   authorized   to   attest   to   the   Mayor ’ s   signature;  and it is further  RESOLVED,   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office  in accordance with applicable law.  _____________________________   ______________________________  Karen Carnevale , R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   September 4, 2018  4846-4355-1600, v. 1

documents.title: SUPPORTING ELECTRIFY JERSEY COALITION AND SUPPORTING ADOPTION OF CLEAN CAR STANDARDS

pages.text: 200-18  September 4, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   is   committed   to   fostering   an  increase   in   the   prevalence   of   electric   vehicles   and   the   expansion   of   infrastructure   for   the   use   of  electric   vehicles   to   reduce   global   warming   pollution   and   improve   air   quality   in   the   Township,   the  State of New Jersey, and the United State of America ; and  WHEREAS,   Electrify   New   Jersey   is   a   coalition   committed   to   transitioning   the  transportation   system   to   promote   the   use   of   electric   vehicles   to   capitalize   on   the   significant  environmental and economic benefits from electric vehicles ; and  WHEREAS,   Electrify   New   Jersey   has   partnered   with   Jersey   Renews,   a   state   coalition  promoting   the   transition   to   clean,   renewable   energy   in   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   and  ChargeEVC,   a   diverse   electric   vehicle   coalition   to   develop   a   comprehensive   framework   to   serve  as   a   roadmap ,   titled   New   Jersey ’ s   Clean   Car   Standards,   to   create   a   sustainable   and   growing  electric vehicle market in New Jersey; and  WHEREAS,   New   Jersey ’ s   Clean   Car   Standards   includes   recommended   goals   and  policy actions designed to:     Expand public charging infrastructure;     Foster greater affordability of EVs to increase adoption;     Ensure sufficient private charging infrastructure;     Ensure electrification solutions to reach all communities equitably;     Ensure long term funding for the transportation trust fund;     Build consumer awareness; and     Sup port market development efforts;  and  WHEREAS,   Governor   Phil   Murphy ’ s   Environment   and   Energy   Transition   Advisory  Committee ’ s   January   1,   2018   recommendations   already   include   electrifying   the   State   of   New  Jersey ’ s transportation system as one of its top priorities ; and

2  WHEREAS,   expanding   the   use   of   electric   vehicles   will   bring   significant   economic  benefits,   including   lower   electricity   rates   by   recreating   a   more   resilient   and   responsive   electric  grid   and   reduced   vehicle   operating   costs,   and   substantial   emission   reductions   that   result   in  cleaner air for everyone; and  WHEREAS,   pending   legislation   exists   with   furthers   the   goals   and   policies   articulated   in  New   Jersey ’ s   Clean   Car   Standards,   including:   (1)   S2252/A3687   which   improves   the   State ’ s  transportation   infrastructure   to   expand   public   charging   stations;   and   (2)   S2382/A3847  establishing   a   rebate   for   the   purchase   of   electric   vehicles   to   encourage   the   residents   to  purchase electric vehicles; and  WHEREAS,   a   template   for   a   proposed   letter   to   Governor   Phil   Murphy   is   annexed   hereto  as   Exhibit   “ A ”   expressing   the   Township ’ s   support   of   both   New   Jersey ’ s   Clean   Car   Standards,  and the pending legislation referenced in this resolution.  NOW   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   hereby   supports   the   implementation   of  New   Jersey ’ s   Clean   Car   Standards   and   the   State   of   New   Jersey ’ s   ability   to   adopt   stronger  safeguards   to   protect   the   health   of   New   Jersey   citizens   above   and   beyond   the   federal  standards ; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Township   opposes   efforts   all   efforts   by   the   federal   government   to  weaken   clean   air   and   emission   standards   or   otherwise   usurp   local   control   to   protect   the  residents   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey   and   the   Township   from   air   pollution   from   the   transportation  sector; and be if further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Township   authorizes   the   Mayor   to   execute   the   letter   to   Governor  Phil   Murphy   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A ”   expressing   the   Township ’ s   support   of   New   Jersey ’ s  Clean Car Standards and the   pending legislation referenced in this resolution ; and be if further

3  RESOLVED,   the   Township   Clerk   is   directed   to   send   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   with   the  executed   letter   to   Governor   Phil   Murphy ;   New   Jersey   State   Senat e   Stephen   M.   Sweeney;   and  the Speaker of the New Jersey Assembly Craig Coughlin; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office  in accordance with applicable law.  ________________________________  Karen   J.   Carnevale , R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   September 4, 2018  4833-0975-3968, v. 1

1  201-18  September 4, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   Rich land   Towers ,   LLC   has   filed   certain   tax   appeals   for   the   years   201 2  through   2014   seeking   a   reduction   of   their   assessment   which   would   reduce   property   taxes   on   their  antenna   tower; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   has   filed   affirmative   appeals   for   the   tax   years  2015 through 2018 ; and  WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   contract,   Blau   &   Blau   handles   all   affirmative   tax   appeal s   a s  special tax counsel ; and  WHEREAS ,   Blau   &   Blau   has   retained   Hannoch   Appraisal   Company   for   the   affirmative  cases ; and  WHEREAS ,   Mark Hendricks,   CRE   generally provides appraisals   for defense cases ; and  WHEREAS ,   special   tax   counsel   has   conferred   with   all   parties   involved   and   determined  it   is   most   efficient   for   Hannoch   Appraisal   Company   to   be   retained   as   the   sole   expert   in   this   case  for the year 201 2   and forward ;   and  WHEREAS,   Hannoch   Appraisal   Company   has   provided   a   proposal   to   split   the   cost s   for  the appraisals and any testimony; and  WHEREAS,   in   2011,   the   assessment   was   $747,700 ,   in   2012   there   was   a   four-month  added assessment based upon a total of $4,754,100; and  WHEREAS,   the total assessment for 2013 through 2018 is $5,321,800; and  WHEREAS,   the taxes in dispute for the years 2012 through 2016 are over $900,000; and  WHEREAS,   special   tax   counsel   has   requested   the   Township   hire   Hannoch   Appraisal  Company   to   handle   the   defense   case   in   order   to   inure   continuity   and   similar   appraisal  methodologies .

2  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   retain  Hannoch   Appraisal   Company   at   a   cost   of   $ 7,500   for   the   2012-2014   tax   appeal   cases .   The  balance   of   all   expenses   including   any   specialist/consultant   in   the   field   of   antenna   towers   will   be  paid   by   the   special   tax   appeal   counsel   in   accordance   with   the   attached   engagement   agreement ;  and it is further  RESOLVED ,   that   a   c opy   of   this   Resolution   and   contract   shall   be   maintained   by   the  Clerk of the Municipal Court and available for inspection by the general public; and it is further  RESOLVED,   that   this   engagement   is   not   made   under   the   Fair   and   Open   Public   Bidding  Laws of the State of New Jersey.  Karen   J.   Carnevale , R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   September 4, 2018  4813-3506-7249, v. 1

documents.title: AUTHORIZING EDWARD BYNRNE MEMORIAL JUSTICE ASSISTANCE GRANT APPLICATION

pages.text: 202-18  September 4, 2018  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   United   States   Department   of   Justice,   through   its   Edward   Byrne  Memorial   Justice   Assistance   Grant   Program ,   has   established   a   grant   award   in   the   amount   of  $11,191.00 ( “ Grant ” ), which is aimed at   officer   safety and wellness in our communities; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Police   Department   ( “ Township ” )   seeks   to  utilize   the   Grant   funds   to   assure   safer   and   enhanced   operations   by   the   WOPD   and   has  submitted an application for the Grant as annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A ” ;   and  WHEREAS ,   as   part   of   the   application,   the   Mayor   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   is  required to execute the Certifications and   Assurances   annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ B; ”   and  NOW,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF  WEST   ORANGE   that   that   the   Township   authorizes   the   application   for   the   Edward   Byrne  Memorial Justice Assistance Grant Program; and be it further  RESOLVED ,   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the   Certifications  and   Assurances   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ B ”   and   any   and   all   documents   needed   for   the  completion of the application;   and   it is further  RESOLVED,   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office  in accordance with applicable law.  .  ________________________________   ________________________________  Karen J. Carnevale , R.M.C.   Susan McCartney  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   September 4, 2018  4836-1362-7249, v. 1

RESOLUTION WHEREAS ,   the   United   States   Department   of   Justice,   through   its   Edward   Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant Program, has established a grant award in the amount of $11,191.00 (“Grant”), which is aimed at officer safety and wellness in our communities; and  WHEREAS , the Township of West Orange Police Department (“Township”) seeks to utilize the Grant funds to assure safer and enhanced operations by the WOPD and has submitted an application for the Grant as annexed hereto as Exhibit   “A” ; and  WHEREAS , as part of the application, the Mayor of the Township of West Orange is required to execute the Certifications and Assurances annexed hereto as Exhibit   “B;”   and  NOW, BE IT RESOLVED BY THE TOWNSHIP COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE   that that the Township authorizes the application for the Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant Program; and be it further  RESOLVED , that the Mayor be and is hereby authorized to execute the Certifications and Assurances annexed hereto as Exhibit   “B”   and any and all documents needed for the completion of the application; and it is further  RESOLVED,   that a copy of this Resolution shall be made available in the Clerk’s office in accordance with applicable law. . ________________________________   ________________________________  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Susan McCartney Township Clerk   Council President Dated: Adopted:  4836-1362-7249, v. 1

PROJECT ABSTRACT  APPLICANT NAME  Town ship   of   West Orange   ( West Orange   Police Department)  Areas Affected by Project:  Township of West Orange  Estimated Funding:  $11,191.00  PROJECT TITLE  Township of   West Orange -   Edward Byrne Memorial Justice   Assistance Grant FY201 8  Application  BRIEF DESCRIPTION  The   Township of West Orange   Police Department   (WOPD)   is applying for funds through the  FY2018   Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant.   The Township’s request aligns with  the   DOJ’s 201 8 JAG pri ority for Officer Safety and Wellness.   With the requested funds, the  WOPD   will improve effectiveness in communications , management, leadership   and overall  efficiency for our officers. This in turn will help all   divisions of our Department   meet   our   goal to  assur e   safer and enhanced operations   by   the WOPD. In addition, the technological equipment  requested   will help employ more effective strategies   for officers   to   aid in   achieving this goal.   By  requesting both training and technology,   the WOPD   will develop a   more   effective, data - driven,  and multi - pronged approach that employs a combination of valuable resources   to effectively  meet its goal .  The   WOPD   is a proactive and response - driven agency that strives to protect and meet the public  safety needs of the   Township of West Orange. Our Department has witnessed many changes  over the years, yet have maintained the traditions of serving the community with the utmost  integrity, while improving efficiency and effectiveness. Our Department advocates for a high  leve l of teamwork within our organization and collaboration with citizens and local groups.  PROJECT IDENTIFIERS  CRIME PREVENTION  DATA SHARNIG LINKAGE  COMMUNICATIONS  EQUIPMENT   --   GENERAL  CONFERENCES AND TRAINING  LICENSE PLATE READERS  OFFICER SAFETY  OFFICER WELLNESS

Township of West Orange -   Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant FY2018 Application  1  P rogram Narrative  A.   Description of the Issues  The Township of West Orange, New Jersey, is the fifth largest municipality in Essex County, with  an area of 12.1 square miles, a population of approximately 47,000, and a daytime population that  swells to about 100,000. Because of the Urban Rim District th at exists along the border of West  Orange and Orange, the Township considers itself a suburban/urban community that serves as the  County’s transition   area   from a largely depressed urban environment to a middle - class suburban  community. The Township has a r acial/ethnic breakdown of approximately 47% White/Non -  Hispanic, 25% Black/African - American, and 19% Hispanic/Latino. About 7.2% of the population  is living at or below the poverty line. According to the 2017 Uniform Crime Report prepared by  the NJ State Po lice, West Orange registered a 9.5% increase in crime compared to 2016.  In March 2011, the Township of West Orange   laid off a total of   eight   officers and demoted 16  others in an effort to plug a $3.4 million budget gap. Based on continued economic hardshi p, the  WOPD ’s   strength of force has remained nearly the same since this time, and the consequences of  these cuts have become apparent. In 2016 alone, the crime rate in W est Orange   rose nearly 18 %  (Uniform Crime Report). Violent crime saw an increase of 7 % .   While the WOPD had a total of 77  violent crimes in 2011, the year the cuts were made, in 2016 the WOPD reported a total of 106.  This represents a 37 %   increase in violent crime in only five years.  This increase runs counter to a general statewide decrease   in violent crime. According to the  Uniform Crime Report Urban - Suburban - Rural Crime 5 - year Comparison 2011/2015 Summary  Analysis, urban and suburban communities in New Jersey experienced the largest   percentage  decrease   in violent crimes, a drop totaling 16 % . The State as a whole saw a decrease in violent  crimes from 2015 to 2016 totaling 4 % , as well as a general decrease in all crime totaling nearly  4 %   (Uniform Crime Report). This represents a positive trend, as violent crime also decreased from  2014 to 2015   by   1% . Violent crime has decreased in several major cities in New Jersey, including  Newark and Camden. Even some communities that saw bumps in violent crime only witnessed  slight increases.   While many   neighboring urban   towns have witnessed decrease s   in vi olent crime,  W est   O range ’s numbers have gone up.  This data points to the fact that   the   WO PD   needs to take a proactive stance   to improve efficiency  and increase capacity for our officer s   to implement a pproaches to counteract   a disproportionate  increase in   crime compared to neighboring communities . To do so,   with this project   the WOPD  will send command - level officers to specific training s   for   l eadership   p rogram s for p ublic   s afety  p ersonnel . With this advanced training, the overall operation of the WOPD will be more efficient  and improve capacity   and therefore safety for   officers.   As a result, t his will maximize our force’s  resources for the benefit of resident safety.   Once funding is secure d , the proposed training and  education project will   commence for the two - year period, as per grant program guidelines.   Two  officers will attend the selected courses   per   semester until all officers complete   th e   course s . The  WOPD has selected the   Executive   Leadership Program for Public Safety Personnel offered by  Fairleigh Dickinson University in New Jersey.   This course is designed for law enforcement/public  safety managers with leadership   a nd command responsibilities .   The WOPD will also send   three  officers   to the   FBI - LEEDA trilogy of courses. This includes the   Supervisor   Leadership Institute,  Command Leadership Institute and Executive Leadership Institute. These courses are utilized

Township of West Orange -   Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant FY2018 Application  2  widely by police departments throughout the country and have proven effectiv e in training officers.  Having command - level officers attend these courses will   effectively enhance the operations of  each Division of the Department   which will improve the safety and wellness of all members of  each Division.   Without these funds, the WOPD   will not be able to send these officers for this  advanced training which is critical to improve efficiency and safety for both officers and residents.  In addition, the WOPD will purchase   two   license plate reader s   that will capture license plate  numbers tha t come into view ,   along with the location, date, and time. The data, which includes  photographs of the vehicle and sometimes its driver and passengers, is then uploaded to a central  server. The WOPD will place the reader s   i n   targeted location s   based upon a reas of the greatest  concern and incidences. This will help the WOPD in its overall operations as we will have an  additional source of information and data tracking   to   help   lessen the man - hours needed   to   search  for specific vehicles and as a result, improv e   overall efficiency of our operations.   T he WOPD will  not be able to   purchase this equipment ,   which   is   critical to improve efficiency   of operations ,  without these funds.  B . Project Design and Implementation  The goal of this project is to improve the capa city of the command staff of the WOPD in an effort  to improve safety and overall wellness for all officers   which in turn will increase the Department’s  efficiency.   The WOPD will utilize FY2018 funding to   sen d four   officers to the Executive  Leadership Program   f or Public Safety Personnel at Fairleigh Dickinson University in New Jersey .  Trainings   will be targeted to   all command officers for different divisions of the Department so that  as many officers as possible are   impacted by this project.   To date,   two   first - line supervisors   of the  Department have already attended this training   which has proven extremely effective. As such,  with these funds we will be able to send an additio nal   four   officers to this training. The t raining  covers the following topics:  •   Leadership, Management and Organizational Culture  •   Civil   Liability/Risk   Management   Internal   A ffairs/Whistle - Blowing   and   Professional  Ethics  •   Interpersonal and Organizational Communication  •   Problem Solving and Decisio n - Making Analysis  •   Labor - Management Relations/Legal Aspects of Discipline, Selection and Promotion  •   Managing Troubled Employees, Mental Health Issues and Critical Incident Stress  •   Planning and Policy Development, Budget Preparation and Management  •   Policing   Diverse Communities with Cultural Sensitivity  •   Police Resource Allocation and Deployment  •   Organizational Change/Group Dynamics and Motivation  •   Public Presentations and Media Strategies and Relations  •   Crime - Fighting Strategies/CompStat/Intelligence - led   Policing  •   Homeland Security, Counterterrorism Strategies and Terrorism Issues and Awareness  The   cost is $1,500   per each   10 - week course ( one day per week )   at FDU. The WOPD will   send  four   officers   to the course over the two - year project period. Two officers will attend per semester  to ensure staff ing levels   are not impacted.

Township of West Orange -   Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant FY2018 Application  3  The WOPD will also send   three   officers to the FBI - LEEDA trilogy of courses including the  Supervisor   Leadership   Institute,   C ommand   Leadership   Institute   and   Executive   Leadership  Institute.   The Supervisor Leadership Institute is geared towards first - line supervisors and middle  managers with the goal of enhancing their leadership competencies. Officers will be engaged in  personali ty diagnostics, leadership case studies, mentoring, developing your people, performance  management, risk management and credibility. The Command Leadership Institute is a dynamic,  intensive and challenging 4   and a   1/2 - day program specifically and uniquely   designed to prepare  law enforcement leaders for command level positions.   The Command Leadership Institute   focuse s  on   provid ing   real life contemporary, best - practice strategies and techniques for those aspiring to  command level assignments.   Officers   will be   engaged in such topics as credibility, command  discipline   and   liability,   dealing   with   problem   employees,   and   leading   change   within   an  organization.   The Executive Leadership Institute is   designed for executive level law enforcement  leaders and focuses on t he emerging challenges facing our profession. This highly - interactive  program follows the FBI - LEEDA “Cops Talking to Cops” model of professional development.  The   course u ses   a wide range of material and calls upon participants’   own professional experience s  to facilitate individual development and learning.   The cost per course is $650 for each of the  LEEDA courses. The WOPD will send   three   officers to the trilogy of courses over the two - year  project period.   ( P lease see attached information on s pecifi c courses).  In addition, funds will be utilized to purchase   two   SM 750C plate reader, Security Enclosure, Solar  power unit s   for approximately $ 1,620   each . Targeted hot spots will be determined and identified  for the placement of the plate reader s   where it will prove most effective and to aid officer efforts.  The WOPD works together with many local stakeholders regularly to identify public safety  perceptions   and feel s   that   this   additional staff training   and equipment   will reflect   these stakeholder  engagement efforts . This investment from the JAG program will al low the WOPD to meet our  unique Department   needs which are currently limited   by   our   in ability to   maximize   our officer s ’  capacity   and   potential.  C . Capabilities and Competencies  The WOPD is a proactive and response - driven agency that strives to protect   and meet the public  safety needs of the Township of West Orange. Our Department has witnessed many changes over  the years, yet ha s   maintained the traditions of serving the community with the utmost integrity  while improving efficiency and effectiveness. Ou r Department advocates for a high level of  teamwork within our organization and collaboration with citizens and local groups. The mission  of the WOPD is to protect the lives, property, and rights of all people; maintain an ethical  environment of mutual res pect, trust and dignity; foster a partnership with the community it serves;  and, improve the quality of life for all within the Township of West Orange.  With this strong commitment to excellence, our agency remains at the forefront of addressing the  chall enges and opportunities of our community. A great part of our success comes from the rapport  and trust that we have built with residents, particularly through our Community Service Unit  (CSU). Established 19 years ago, the CSU is a well - established unit wi th dedicated and  experienced officers. In addition to the CSU, the WOPD’s Juvenile Aid Bureau (JAB) carries the  Department’s mission to the heart of its programming by respecting youths as contributing  members of the community, ensuring that their rights a re protected and offering intervention

Township of West Orange -   Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant FY2018 Application  4  services. The most effective form of prevention is through education and early intervention, and  to this end, the Bureau provides educational programs to all groups within the community.  The proposed project will be l ed by   Chief James P. Abbott   who   has supervised every area of the  WOPD and has spent the previous 21 years as Chief of the Department. With nearly   38   years of  experience in Law Enforcement, Chief Abbott   has the capability and competency to effectively  carry   out this project.   T o   assure   quality, the efforts conducted   will be quantified by accurate and  up - to - date crime statistics/data compiled throughout the grant period.   The WOPD will take a  proactive approach to ensure that insights into both crime levels and   community attitudes about  area safety are considered as part of this project.  D . Plan for Collecting the Data Required for this Solicitation’s Performance Measures  The WOPD will designate a single contact to manage and report data, (i.e. progress with  pe rformance measures based on their request, finances expended, finances drawn - down, etc.) .   This  data will be collected from officer reports, community surveys, and crime statistics. Accurate data  retrieval and analysis will enable us to maximize operational   efficacy and meet the target goal of  maximizing our capacity and improving efficiency for overall safety and wellness of the WOPD.  All data will be collected and communicated to the US DOJ through the Grants Management  System (GMS) or other requested form s.   Quarterly performance reports will be submitted utilizing  the BJA’s Performance Measurement Tool (PMT). Internal communications and reporting  protocol within the Department will be established and the designated point of contact will   as sure  all officers   follow this protocol.   This will ensure that all data and information is reported in a  timely fashion and that all required reports will be completed and submitted to the US DOJ in a  timely manner.

Leadership Integrity  This two day seminar uses both classic and contemporary ethical decision-making procedures to help students identify ethical issues and take corrective action.  Media & Public Relations  This four and one half day intensive course is created to provide a strong foundation of best-practice training for both PIOs and police leaders.  Master PIO  This four and one half day intensive course is created build off of the FBI-LEEDA Media & Public Relations course. The Master Public Information Officer course moves the participant from the tactical to the strategic level of media and brand management.
